https://www.adzuna.be/details/5587533879.scraped: The Global Systems Administrator is accountable for operational and project related activities, and maintaining defined IT standards at all global IKO locations. Scope includes global Data Centre and all remote site data rooms, cloud solutions, servers, storage, backup operations and disaster recovery solutions. Incumbent will apply excellent communication, problem-solving, change management, and systems administration skills to ensure high quality operations. This role is based in Antwerp, Belgium. WHEN YOU JOIN US, YOU WILL BE. Closely adheres to defined Global IT Service Management processes (when published) – change management, incident management, problem management, service level management, knowledge management and request fulfillment processes Partners with IT staff, service providers, and end-users to support and maintain IKO’s complex and quickly growing core infrastructure. Provide level 2 or 3 support, and above, for any global core infrastructure issue in line with defined SLA’s and OLA’s. Hands over unresolved critical/high priority incidents to other global team members with detailed supporting documentation at the end of normal working hours or under coordination of Manager if for an off-hours incident. Scheduled for support in on-call rotation and, for the period scheduled, is expected to respond (within SLA) and troubleshoot incidents for any global location outside of business-hours. For example, during the night and at weekends. Responsible for the uptime, resiliency and performance of all core infrastructure with a focus on ERP systems including Oracle JD Edwards Help manage, optimize and plan capacity for IKO’s entire core ERP infrastructure including storage environments. Creation and maintenance of published documentation for technical procedures, policies, and standard processes related to core infrastructure Collaborates with project team members, providing technical expertise, assist in creation of detailed task plans and executes activities, communicating status accordingly Research new technologies to stay informed on core infrastructure industry standards and best practices. Makes recommendations to Manager, Global Core Infrastructure for inclusion in technology roadmap Implement, administer, and monitor security solutions, standards, and policies in accordance with IKO’s Information Security Framework. Investigates possible security breaches as part of incident management team Additional duties as assigned by the Manager, Global Core Infrastructure OUR IDEAL CANDIDATE. Bachelor's degree or equivalent in Information Technology, Computer Science, Engineering or related field. 5-10 years’ experience managing and maintaining datacenter systems, processes and systems architecture in large enterprises, ideally in support of ERP systems Effective leader of ideas and people with a deep understanding of business capabilities and processes Broad industry experience and domain expertise –industrial manufacturing sector experience an asset Ability to assess datacenter trends in order to develop and implement hardware and software standards for network Collaborative team player who can effectively participate in departmental level meetings and positively influence outcomes Solid understanding of licensing models and practice, enterprise inventory tools, all to ensure compliance is maintained. Oracle license experience beneficial. Upholds 'best practice' standards as well as departmental policies and procedures Excellent communication and presentations skills Excellent problem-solving skill Able to travel globally to supported locations (up to 25%) Additional Key Skills/Experience: Oracle Enterprise Linux, Red Hat Enterprise Linux (5.x, 6.x, 7.x) Oracle JD Edwards, WebLogic, WebCenter, DB experience an asset VMWare (ESX 6.x) in an large enterprise multi-site environment in support of Oracle ERP/DB Windows server 2012, 2016, 2019 NFS/NAS/Distributed File Shares Virtual machine and Oracle DB Backup methods and functions (CommVault knowledge an asset) Microsoft Active Directory Management Enterprise Anti-Virus HP Server Management (Proliant/C7000/Synergy/OneView) Enterprise System Monitoring Ticket entry and change management experience #LI-SM2

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5607116360.scraped: The Job As a Data Platform Engineer, you are the backbone of data ecosystems. You create scalable, resilient platforms that empower data engineers, analysts, and scientists to turn raw data into meaningful insights fast, safely and with confidence. You take ownership of the underlying infrastructure, tooling, and standards that make data work at scale, balancing performance, cost and reliability. From cloud resources and orchestration tot data governance and observability. You ensure the platform is robust today and ready for tomorrow’s use cases, enabling teams to focus on creating value from data rather than managing complexity. At Dataroots , our mission is to innovate, design, and deliver robust data solutions and platforms for a variety of sectors. Our growing team operates out of Leuven and Ghent, where our dedicated in-house data & AI specialists apply state-of-the-art methodologies to optimize and transform our clients' business processes. Our strong emphasis on DataOps and MLOps means we build solutions that are not only intelligent but also production-ready, offering clients a solid foundation to make data-driven decisions with confidence. . What you can expect at Dataroots? Dataroots was founded in 2016 and, since its beginnings, has focused on helping its customers achieve excellence in their data-driven operations. The only way to do this sustainably is by investing in team well-being, diversity, knowledge sharing and customer experience. This quality-first focus has allowed sustained and healthy growth over the years. Join a team where you can have an impact on our growth story. Read up more on our DNA over at [https://dataroots.io/our-dna]. The Skills Data Platform And Cloud Engineering skills: Expertise in data engineering and cloud architecture. Proficiency in building ELT data pipelines using tools like Airflow, Prefect, Dagster, Spark, dbt, or related tooling. Mastery of software engineering skills including Git, Python, and SQL. Expertise in cloud engineering with tooling like Terraform, Docker, Kubernetes, … for robust and scalable cloud infrastructure. Understanding of at least one data platform such as Databricks, Snowflake, Fabric, Azure data services, or AWS data services. The Offer An attractive salary with extralegal benefits, including: Mobility budget or a company car with fuel/charging card Hospitalization and group insurance High-end laptop Smartphone with subscription Substantial amount of holidays Meal vouchers ... Diverse and welcoming work environment , where you’ll collaborate & unwind with colleagues from different cultures and disciplines. The organization of both fun & professional events and initiatives is actively encouraged and supported. A training budget for individual and team learning opportunities. Tons of team-building events and sports initiatives to stay connected and unwind. Where you’ll work Our main offices are in Leuven and Ghent , with co-working spaces in Charleroi and Antwerp . These spaces are perfect for both focusing and unwinding with colleagues, as well as brainstorming sessions and team events. While much of your work can be performed remotely, you will also work on-site with clients. Ready to join Dataroots? Excited to shape the future as a Data Platform Engineer with us? We’re looking forward to hearing from you! Apply now and start a new chapter with Dataroots!

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5614812723.scraped: Job Description At TOMRA Food, the Systems & Sensors team takes ownership of how all components in our machines work together. In addition, we focus on the quality of inspection by designing cutting-edge vision systems to unlock the ultimate sorting capability, ensure quality, minimise waste and maximise customer value. Together with the other teams in R&D, we are proud to own the complexity and deliver the breakthrough. Your mission: As a Systems Engineer , in our office in Leuven , you are the technical linchpin that connects innovative R&D concepts to robust, market-leading machine performance. You will: Take the technical lead in the end-to-end design, configuration, installation and testing of our roadmap sorting machines. Innovate vision systems: Actively support the research and development of optical systems and sensor technologies, pushing boundaries of inspection quality. Drive optimisation: utilise your hands on approach to build test-setups, rigorously collect and analyse data and document findings to drive machine improvements. Complete technical documentation: Develop and maintain documentation for system architecture, configuration and operational procedures. Have cross-functional impact: Collaborate across a vast technical landscape including optics, automation, production, supply chain and service. Provide technical guidance and demonstrate leadership.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5611767270.scraped: Description de l'emploi Nous recrutons un.e Automation Engineer pour une entreprise de la région de Vielsalm. En tant qu’Automation Engineer, vous êtes responsable du suivi du parc d’automates et du réseau de communication, en veillant au respect des standards de sécurité, de la culture « Zero Harm », de la qualité et des normes environnementales. Votre mission principale est d’assurer la fiabilité et l’optimisation des systèmes automatisés tout en apportant un support technique aux équipes. Vos responsabilités : - Identifier les besoins d’amélioration pour maintenir ou accroître la fiabilité des systèmes. - Analyser et résoudre les dysfonctionnements techniques. - Optimiser les interfaces HMI en fonction des besoins de la production et des erreurs détectées. - Participer aux projets d’installation de nouveaux équipements : validation technique et intégration dans le réseau existant. - Développer et tester les interfaces machines pour intégrer les nouveaux équipements. - Définir et assurer le suivi des règles de back-up et des pontages. - Investiguer les sources d’amélioration et proposer des solutions innovantes. - Gérer les stocks de pièces de rechange (cartes mémoires…) et garantir leur disponibilité. - Former le personnel dans votre domaine de compétences. - Être l’interlocuteur privilégié du service IT. - Assurer une communication claire et fiable avec la hiérarchie, les collègues, les techniciens et les opérateurs.Profil- Diplôme minimum : Bachelier en automation avec une expérience confirmée en milieu industriel. - Expertise technique en électricité et automation. - Maîtrise des systèmes Siemens (S7 400, S7 1500, Simatic Manager, TIA Portal). - Connaissance des protocoles Profibus, Profinet, communication inter-PLC. - Expérience avec Scada WinCC ou Wonderware. - Atouts supplémentaires : PLC Beckhoff TwinCat, variateurs ABB et SEW, matériel Safety, S5, programmation List. - Bonnes capacités de communication, sens des responsabilités, autonomie. - Excellentes compétences d’analyse, résolution de problèmes et esprit d’initiative. - Maîtrise des outils informatiques (Suite Office, ERP SAP). - Connaissance linguistique : anglais requis.OffreNotre client vous propose : - Un contrat à durée indéterminée dans un environnement industriel innovant. - Un salaire compétitif complété par des avantages extra-légaux. - La possibilité de leasing vélo. - Des formations pour soutenir votre développement professionnel. - De réelles perspectives d’évolution de carrière. - Une stratégie claire en matière de durabilité (pour la planète, les clients et les collaborateurs). - Des réductions intéressantes sur les produits de l’entreprise. - Des avantages exclusifs via la plateforme benefits@work. Intéressé.e par ce poste d'Automation Engineer ? Postulez vite en ligne ! Vous avez des questions ? Envoyez un mail à julie.dantinne@adecco.be. A bientôt chez Adecco, Julie Dantinne

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5611216560.scraped: We're working with a gaming company based in Brussels who are looking for an Infrastructure Engineer to help build and evolve the systems that keep their games online and their teams moving fast. This role is heavily involved in the technical platform: supporting cloud and On-Prem infrastructure, improving automation, and making sure environments are secure. It's hands-on, varied, and ideal for someone who enjoys both building and maintaining systems. Responsibilities Designing, deploying, and maintaining cloud and On-Prem infrastructure Managing AWS environments, focusing on core compute and Storage services Operating and improving virtualized and containerized platforms (ideally Proxmox) Maintaining and supporting Linux (Debian-based) and Windows systems, including server and Embedded environments Building and maintaining infrastructure using TerraForm and Ansible Writing and maintaining scripts in Python, Bash, and/or PowerShell to automate operations Supporting and improving CI/CD pipelines, with a solid understanding of how delivery workflows function Troubleshooting infrastructure issues and improving system reliability and performance Collaborating closely with development and technical teams to support game platforms Applying basic networking knowledge to diagnose and resolve connectivity or performance issues Your profile AWS, with solid knowledge of classic compute and Storage services Virtualization and containers, ideally with Proxmox Linux environments, preferably Debian-based (server and Embedded applications) Windows systems, including server and Embedded use cases Infrastructure as Code & automation using TerraForm and Ansible Scripting skills in Python and/or Bash (PowerShell is a plus) CI/CD concepts, with a good understanding of how pipelines work Basic networking knowledge is a plus The offer A chance to work in the gaming industry, where performance and uptime really matter A mix of cloud and On-Prem infrastructure, not just one or the other Real ownership over systems and automation, not just maintenance A collaborative technical team that values practical solutions over buzzwords Flexible hybrid work Up to EUR6.500 (dependent on experience & interview process) Full package including mobility options/car

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5608563120.scraped: At Capgemini Engineering, the world leader in engineering services, we bring together a global team of engineers, scientists, and architects to help the world's most innovative companies unleash their potential. From autonomous cars to life-saving robots, our digital and software technology experts think outside the box as they provide unique R&D and engineering services across all industries. Join us for a career full of opportunities. Where you can make a difference. Where no two days are the same. Your role As an Automation Engineer , you will play a pivotal role in designing, implementing, and optimizing industrial automation systems with a focus on data integration and analytics, particularly in GMP-regulated environments. In this role you will play a key role in: Designing and implementing PLC/SCADA systems and industrial networks (Modbus, Profinet, Profibus, Ethernet/IP) for manufacturing clients in pharmaceutical and other regulated industries. Developing GMP-compliant automation solutions including programming, validation, and documentation that meet strict regulatory requirements. Creating and optimizing data historian systems (WinCC, Ignition, OSIsoft PI) to enable data-driven decision making and process improvement. Implementing Industrial IoT architectures using OPC UA, MQTT, and edge computing technologies to connect shop floor systems with enterprise applications. Supporting FAT/SAT execution, validation activities, and troubleshooting for complex automation projects. Collaborating with cross-functional teams including production, quality, and IT to deliver integrated automation solutions. Your profile Bachelor's or Master's degree in Automation, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering or related field. 7+ years of experience in industrial automation with focus on data systems in regulated environments. Strong expertise in PLC programming (preferably Siemens TIA Portal) and HMI/SCADA development (WinCC). Proven experience with industrial communication protocols and networking (Profinet, Profibus, Modbus, Ethernet/IP). Hands-on experience implementing data historian systems and industrial IoT solutions. Knowledge of GMP compliance requirements and validation processes for automated systems. Fluent in English and French. We help our clients unleashing their R&D potential to develop smart products and services of tomorrow. About Capgemini Capgemini is a global business and technology transformation partner, helping organizations to accelerate their dual transition to a digital and sustainable world, while creating tangible impact for enterprises and society. It is a responsible and diverse group of 340,000 team members in more than 50 countries. With its strong over 55-year heritage, Capgemini is trusted by its clients to unlock the value of technology to address the entire breadth of their business needs. It delivers end-to-end services and solutions leveraging strengths from strategy and design to engineering, all fuelled by its market leading capabilities in AI, generative AI, cloud and data, combined with its deep industry expertise and partner ecosystem.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5580512942.scraped: 360dialog is the leading Whatsapp platform for Independent Software Providers. We are hosting the Whatsapp channels for more than 800 software solutions and 45000+ B2B clients. As a bootstrapped and profitable company with no investors in the cap table, we are free to do what makes sense from the business as well as from the tech perspective. Our mission is to enable businesses to not only adapt messaging channels but also use them for performance use cases and drive revenue through them. We love to use state of the art engineering methods all over the place, in software development, tech operations but you will find them also in non-tech areas through the whole organization. We are looking for like-minded people to join our organization and contribute with their ideas, skills and expertise in exchange for a good payment. What we offer: Here’s an opportunity to shape a whole new industry in Business Messaging. You’ll be joining a team of experienced entrepreneurs who run a bootstrapped and profitable global company. Join a dynamic, innovative team where you'll collaborate with some of the brightest minds in tech. Enjoy daily opportunities to learn, grow, and push the boundaries of what's possible. Work from anywhere in the world with full remote flexibility. Receive a competitive salary package that reflects your skills, experience and achievements. NB: This is a contract-based position. We are a fully remote company and welcome applicants from anywhere in the world—please disregard the listed countries, which are included only due to system requirements. Requirements Key Requirements 4+ years in DevOps / SRE / Cloud Engineering. Proactive, independent ownership — you anticipate issues, make clear trade-offs, and drive outcomes without hand-holding. Deep Terraform experience (modules, tests, structure, scalability). Production GitOps (ArgoCD strongly preferred). Solid Kubernetes (GKE ideal; multi-cluster a plus). Modern CI/CD experience (any tool). Hands-on with Vault, Prometheus, Grafana . Ability to code for infra (Python, or Go, Bash). Fluent English, growth mindset , and hunger to learn. Budget The budget range for this role is €5.000,00/Month – €10.000,00/Month, depending on the candidate’s level of experience and fit with the position requirements. Background Check Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge and agree that 360Dialog may conduct a background check as part of the recruitment and selection process. This may include, verification of your professional experience, educational background, and other relevant information necessary to assess your suitability for the role.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5605572885.scraped: About the Company: Haulogy is on a mission to digitalize the energy sector and speed up the green energy transition while lowering energy costs for everyone. We are successfully impacting this transition today by providing energy and utility market players with a modular and scalable management software and relevant business and IT services in a long-term partnership approach. This position is available within our New Solutions department, which focuses on creating innovative solutions that power the energy transition. We are a team of experts dedicated to building cutting-edge products that address the evolving needs of the energy market, fostering a culture of innovation , efficiency , and deep business expertise . The Product and Team: Our department develops and manages a portfolio of advanced software solutions designed to harvest and optimize the flexibility offered by renewable production and consumption in energy applications: Flex.Guard: A platform that enables electricity grid operators to dynamically manage network constraints and increase the grid's capacity to host renewable energy sources. Flex.Boost: An AI-based software that controls flexible energy processes (like battery storage, industrial loads, and EV charging) to optimize energy costs against volatile market prices and maximize self-consumption. Our teams are organized into empowered, autonomous "squads" . Each squad is a cross-functional unit that owns specific business domains, allowing them to develop a deep understanding of customer needs and deliver value in short, rapid iterations. You will join one of these squads as a key engineering contributor. The Role: We’re looking for a Software or Applied Math Engineer with a strong background in Power Systems who wants to work at the intersection of electricity networks, energy optimization, and real-world software products. In this role, you will turn complex energy system models and algorithms into production-ready software that supports electricity grid operations and energy optimization that make a real impact on the energy transition. Responsibilities: As a Power System Software Engineer, you will be a key member of our development team, combining technical expertise with sound engineering practices to build software that delivers real value to users. Energy System Modeling: Design and implement algorithms for electricity grid management, with a strong focus on network constraints, capacity optimization, and system reliability. Production-Ready Code Development: Translate advanced power system concepts into software implementation that operates at scale in the field. Team Collaboration: Work within a cross-functional squad (engineering, product, energy experts) to deliver value in fast, iterative cycles. Qualifications: To excel in this role, you should possess the following qualifications: Educational Background: A Master’s degree or PhD in Applied Mathematics, Electrical Engineering, Power Systems, or a related field with a strong focus on IT/Software. Domain Knowledge: Deep understanding of electrical grid operations and optimization. Technical Expertise: Proficiency in Python is required. Ability and desire to learn new technologies, programming languages and best coding practices. Collaboration Tools: Familiarity with collaborative software development practices using Git/GitHub or similar platforms. Problem-Solving Skills: Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to tackle complex technical challenges. Additional Desired Qualifications: Comfortable engaging with product and domain stakeholders to ensure technical solutions address real customer challenges. Professional proficiency in English. Fluency in French is a plus.

Production-Ready Code Development: Translate advanced power system concepts into software implementation that operates at scale in the field. Team Collaboration: Work within a cross-functional squad (engineering, product, energy experts) to deliver value in fast, iterative cycles. Qualifications: To excel in this role, you should possess the following qualifications: Educational Background: A Master’s degree or PhD in Applied Mathematics, Electrical Engineering, Power Systems, or a related field with a strong focus on IT/Software. Domain Knowledge: Deep understanding of electrical grid operations and optimization. Technical Expertise: Proficiency in Python is required. Ability and desire to learn new technologies, programming languages and best coding practices. Collaboration Tools: Familiarity with collaborative software development practices using Git/GitHub or similar platforms. Problem-Solving Skills: Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to tackle complex technical challenges. Additional Desired Qualifications: Comfortable engaging with product and domain stakeholders to ensure technical solutions address real customer challenges. Professional proficiency in English. Fluency in French is a plus.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5599589212.scraped: Are you passionate about cybersecurity and cloud technologies? Do you thrive on solving complex challenges and building secure, automated solutions? We are looking for a Cloud Security Engineer to help strengthen our growing Cloud SOC team. As part of our Managed SOC offering, we deliver a full-service Security Operations Center to customers. In this engineering role, you’ll go beyond monitoring—you’ll design, automate, and optimize security services that make a real impact. As a Cloud Security Engineer, you will: Set up and configure new customers in our SOC environment. Build workflows and integrations to streamline security operations. Optimize detection rules and create advanced threat detection logic. Explore and implement AI-driven enhancements for security processes. Contribute to new services like Vulnerability Management as a Service (VMaaS) and External Attack Surface Management (EASM). Share ideas, suggest improvements, and help shape the future of our SOC. We’re looking for someone with: A degree in IT and ideally 1–2 years of experience in infrastructure or security engineering. Familiarity with Microsoft technologies and cloud security concepts. Automation & Infrastructure: Logic Apps, Terraform, Azure Security Tools: Microsoft Sentinel, Defender XDR Scripting: Python, PowerShell, Bash Strong problem-solving skills, eager to learn and contribute. Fluent in Dutch & English, French is a plus. You are a true team player who values knowledge sharing and collective success. At SecWise, we believe that security is serious business—but work should still be fun. We’re a tight‑knit team within the Cronos Group, and as a proud member of the Microsoft Intelligent Security Association (MISA), we stay at the forefront of modern security innovation. Knowledge sharing, personal growth, and a healthy work‑life balance are part of our DNA. Here’s what we offer: A competitive salary package with a company car. Great benefits through the Cronos Group care package. A flat, open culture where your ideas matter. Support to grow into the best version of yourself. Ready to engineer the future of cloud security? Apply now or reach out to learn more about how you can make an impact at SecWise. Download attachment(s) This job as a PDF file

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5598849523.scraped: Actemium, marque de VINCI Energies Belgium, entièrement dédiée à l'Industrie est aussi leader dans la fourniture de solutions et de services à l'Industrie. Notre focus, l'amélioration des processus industriels chez nos clients. Dans le cadre de notre croissance en Wallonie, nous recherchons un Automation Engineer avec une solide expérience en processus industriels et un esprit d'équipe fort. Au sein d'une équipe projets, vous travaillez de manière autonome à des solutions sur mesure chez nos clients actifs en Wallonie et issus exclusivement du secteur industriel. Responsabilités Programmer et implémenter des systèmes PLC, SCADA ou DCS chez nos clients Intégrer et paramétrer des systèmes IT industriels Analyser les process et réfléchir, le cas échéant, à la mise au point de nouveaux concepts Réaliser des analyses fonctionnelles adaptées aux situations techniques rencontrées Implémenter les fonctionnalités et réaliser les mises en service chez nos clients Rédiger la documentation technique de la solution Supporter/exécuter les processus de validation du système Profil Master en Sciences de l’Ingénieur Industriel, Master/Bachelor Automation Idéalement 5 ans d'expérience en automatisation industrielle, une expérience dans l’industrie de process constitue un réel atout Connaissances en automates programmables Siemens (S7, TIA, PCS7) Connaissances en systèmes de supervision SCADA (WinCC, InTouch, Aveva System Platform) constitue un sérieux atout Esprit analytoque fort et rigueur (respect des délais et des normes de sécurité) Flexible et curieux, vous adoptez une attitude « problem-solving » en toute circonstance Francophone, vous êtes à l’aise en anglais Notre offre Actemium fait partie du groupe VINCI Energies. Chez nous, l’accent est mis sur : Des projets innovants L’Esprit d’équipe, l’implication dans l’organisation et le développement personnel Une formation sur mesure pour encadrer de manière optimale vos débuts chez nous Un parcours de carrière clair avec vue sur votre avenir et des perspectives d’évolution Un package salarial complet avec voiture de société, bonus, flexibilité et avantages extra-légaux De nombreuses initiatives autour du partage de connaissances, des activités sportives, familiales et culturelles ainsi que de cohésion d'équipes

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5598849535.scraped: As an Integration and Automation Engineer, you'll be the technical powerhouse behind our client projects. Your responsibility will be to design, build and maintain integrations that enable enterprise systems to communicate efficiently, securely, and at scale. You'll also collaborate with multiple stakeholders to streamline and automate business processes. What drives you? We're seeking a community-oriented engineer who excels at solving complex integration problems, collaborating with colleagues, and exchanging knowledge. You have a genuine passion for technology and innovation, and you turn that energy into meaningful results for clients. You thrive on advising clients, steering them through technical decisions and implementations. What do you bring to the table? Problem-solving mindset with excellent communication and collaboration skills. Experience with leading integration/iPaaS platforms Workato, Boomi, MuleSoft, etc.). In-depth knowledge of APIs REST, SOAP, GraphQL, etc.), messaging technologies JMS, AMQP, Kafka, etc.), and other integration patterns. Track record integrating large enterprise IT systems SAP, Dynamics, NetSuite, Salesforce, etc.). Experience in one or more cloud platforms Azure, AWS, or GCP. Fluent in English. (Dutch and/or French is a plus). Nice to have: Experience with Kubernetes and DevOps Technologies. Ability to translate business requirements into technical solutions. Experience in business process modeling and automation. What will you be doing? You primary focus will be designing and implementing robust integration solutions, but your role extends beyond coding. You will also work closely with business stakeholders to align on process requirements and design business automation solutions. Design, develop, and test complex integration scenarios using leading iPaaS platforms and resilient integration patterns. Model and optimize business processes , working with business analysts to translate requirements into automated workflows. Lead integration-centered projects and architectures , including API Management, Data Integration, Application Integration, EDA, and B2B Integration. Work with a diverse range of clients , including public sector organizations and international enterprises. Always taking a pragmatic approach. Grow as an expert by focusing on knowledge, absorbing the experience from peers, and actively contributing to the i8c community. What do we offer? Become part of the biggest integration community in Belgium : you'll find a group of like-minded integration experts, each with their own areas of interest and expertise. From Event-driven Architecture and API Management to security and DevOps, we all share a passion for knowledge sharing and technical discussions. You're in the driver's seat of your career path : your career is in your hands. Whether you aspire to be an entrepreneur or a specialist, we are here to support your ambitions. Continuous learning opportunities : you get the opportunity to collaborate on innovative initiatives and expand your knowledge, and we provide access to training to guide you on your path. Enjoy the freedom to work where you're most productive : our team operates in a hybrid mode, splitting time between the customer's sites, our offices in Kontich, Ghent, and at home. Great remuneration package : you can count on a competitive salary along with numerous extra-legal benefits (company car, fuel card, laptop, phone, mobile subscription, insurance,...)

Support daily operations, planning activities, and project work by responding to requests from internal teams and external partners. Actively contribute to continuous improvement initiatives, process optimization, and knowledge sharing within the team. Ability to work independently, methodically, and responsibly, with strong prioritization and time management skills. Excellent analytical and communication abilities to assess impact and risk and communicate effectively. https://www.adzuna.be/details/5598849231.scraped: Why we approach Applied Observability from within an integration point of view? Simple. Integration is where context lives. Integration stitches APIs, events, data contracts and workflows into one living system. Start observability here and you see end-to-end truth instead of isolated noise . In a landscape where AI, automation, composable architectures and quantum computing constantly redraw the playing field, that context makes all the difference. It enables organizations not just to react to what happens, but to anticipate what comes next . We strongly believe Applied Observability is the strategic lens for foresight. Together with specialists like you, we bring it to life aided with our integration background to help organizations not only keep pace with disruption, but stay ahead of it. WHO ARE WE? Hi, we are Archers! Nice to meet you. We’re a community of 25 integration architects and analysts within CornerBriX , Belgium’s leading integration community. It’s quite simple: if you love sharing knowledge, having your insights challenged, and experimenting with technology, you’ll feel right at home with us. You’ll join a community that challenges existing digital systems. Dares to ask the right questions. That loves to think critically about technological innovations. Our Archers Knowledge Days (AKDs) are the perfect example: community days dedicated to exchanging lessons from projects, conferences and new tech so you can level up fast. WHAT DOES YOUR JOB AS AN APPLIED OBSERVABILITY SPECIALIST AT ARCHERS LOOK LIKE You'll drive observability initiatives together with our community of integration experts by implementing the Archers Observability Program for leading enterprises. You’ll design intelligent alerting and visualization mechanism that deliver real business value by capturing traces, logs and metrics from integrated systems. You’ll analyze opportunities across organizational layers to strenghten observability capabilities and prepare systems for the AId)riven future. You'll design and implement monitoring and troubleshooting solutions that ensure seamless integrations to underpin resilient system landscapes. You'll collaborate with architects, developers and operations to embed observability best practices across cross-functional teams. You'll mentor and coach customers in the Archers methodology and frameworks through seminars, training and direct guidance so they can sustain and evolve observability maturity. WHAT WE EXPECT FROM APPLIED OBSERVABILITY SPECIALIST You are familiar with one or more observability and logging & monitoring technologies like Grafana, Prometheus, Dynatrace, Graylog, Datadog, AppDynamics, New Relic, Elastic, Splunk, Instana,... You have hands-on experience with the architecture and design of integrations, services, APIs, etc. You have experience with architecture/design modeling languages (such as Archimate and UML) and tools such as Sparx Enterprise Architect or Archi. You have experience with one or more interface technologies and concepts : XSD, WSDL, SOAP, REST, Swagger, OpenAPI, JSON, and others. WHAT WE OFFER OUR APPLIED OBSERVABILITY SPECIALIST Be part of the leading integration community: As part of CornerBriX , you'll join Belgium's leading integration community , certified as both a Great Place to Work and a Best Workplace . You'll connect with more than 180 experts, with knowledge ranging from business and data architecture to platform engineering, iPaaS, cloud-native integration platfroms and much more. Sustainability and wellbeing : We care about people and the world around us. From health to environment and society, we act with impact guided by the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Continuous learning : Your growth and job happiness come first. We tackle challenging projects together, challenge each other's thinking, discuss emerging technologies, and help you become as happy as you can be in your career . Room to experiment and innovate : We stay ahead by giving you space to explore what's new . You'll always have the time and tools to sharpen your skills, try out new technologies, and lead in a fast-moving world. Whenever you need it. Hybrid working : Flexibility is part of how we work, whether you're collaborating at a customers site, connecting form our offices in Kontich or Ghent, or working from home in line with customer policies. Competitive package : Count on a competitive remuneration package including a company car, charging pass, laptop, phone, subscription and insurance.

It enables organizations not just to react to what happens, but to anticipate what comes next . We strongly believe Applied Observability is the strategic lens for foresight. Together with specialists like you, we bring it to life aided with our integration background to help organizations not only keep pace with disruption, but stay ahead of it. WHO ARE WE? Hi, we are Archers! Nice to meet you. We’re a community of 25 integration architects and analysts within CornerBriX , Belgium’s leading integration community. It’s quite simple: if you love sharing knowledge, having your insights challenged, and experimenting with technology, you’ll feel right at home with us. You’ll join a community that challenges existing digital systems. Dares to ask the right questions. That loves to think critically about technological innovations. Our Archers Knowledge Days (AKDs) are the perfect example: community days dedicated to exchanging lessons from projects, conferences and new tech so you can level up fast. WHAT DOES YOUR JOB AS AN APPLIED OBSERVABILITY SPECIALIST AT ARCHERS LOOK LIKE You'll drive observability initiatives together with our community of integration experts by implementing the Archers Observability Program for leading enterprises. You’ll design intelligent alerting and visualization mechanism that deliver real business value by capturing traces, logs and metrics from integrated systems. You’ll analyze opportunities across organizational layers to strenghten observability capabilities and prepare systems for the AId)riven future. You'll design and implement monitoring and troubleshooting solutions that ensure seamless integrations to underpin resilient system landscapes. You'll collaborate with architects, developers and operations to embed observability best practices across cross-functional teams. You'll mentor and coach customers in the Archers methodology and frameworks through seminars, training and direct guidance so they can sustain and evolve observability maturity. WHAT WE EXPECT FROM APPLIED OBSERVABILITY SPECIALIST You are familiar with one or more observability and logging & monitoring technologies like Grafana, Prometheus, Dynatrace, Graylog, Datadog, AppDynamics, New Relic, Elastic, Splunk, Instana,... You have hands-on experience with the architecture and design of integrations, services, APIs, etc. You have experience with architecture/design modeling languages (such as Archimate and UML) and tools such as Sparx Enterprise Architect or Archi. You have experience with one or more interface technologies and concepts : XSD, WSDL, SOAP, REST, Swagger, OpenAPI, JSON, and others. WHAT WE OFFER OUR APPLIED OBSERVABILITY SPECIALIST Be part of the leading integration community: As part of CornerBriX , you'll join Belgium's leading integration community , certified as both a Great Place to Work and a Best Workplace . You'll connect with more than 180 experts, with knowledge ranging from business and data architecture to platform engineering, iPaaS, cloud-native integration platfroms and much more. Sustainability and wellbeing : We care about people and the world around us. From health to environment and society, we act with impact guided by the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Continuous learning : Your growth and job happiness come first. We tackle challenging projects together, challenge each other's thinking, discuss emerging technologies, and help you become as happy as you can be in your career . Room to experiment and innovate : We stay ahead by giving you space to explore what's new . You'll always have the time and tools to sharpen your skills, try out new technologies, and lead in a fast-moving world. Whenever you need it. Hybrid working : Flexibility is part of how we work, whether you're collaborating at a customers site, connecting form our offices in Kontich or Ghent, or working from home in line with customer policies. Competitive package : Count on a competitive remuneration package including a company car, charging pass, laptop, phone, subscription and insurance.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5598848774.scraped: Are you passionate about cybersecurity and cloud technologies? Do you thrive on solving complex challenges and building secure, automated solutions? At SecWise , a proud member of the Microsoft Intelligent Security Association (MISA), we’re looking for a Cloud Security Engineer to strengthen our growing Cloud SOC team in Leuven. As part of our Managed SOC offering, we deliver a full-service Security Operations Center to our customers. In this engineering role, you’ll go beyond monitoring—you’ll design, automate, and optimize security services that make a real impact. As a Cloud Security Engineer, you will: Set up and configure new customers in our SOC environment. Build workflows and integrations to streamline security operations. Optimize detection rules and create advanced threat detection logic. Explore and implement AI-driven enhancements for security processes. Contribute to new services like Vulnerability Management as a Service (VMaaS) and External Attack Surface Management (EASM). Share ideas, suggest improvements, and help shape the future of our SOC. We’re looking for someone with: A degree in IT and ideally 1–2 years of experience in infrastructure or security engineering. Familiarity with Microsoft technologies and cloud security concepts. Automation & Infrastructure: Logic Apps, Terraform, Azure Security Tools: Microsoft Sentinel, Defender XDR Scripting: Python, PowerShell, Bash Strong problem-solving skills, eager to learn and contribute. Fluent in Dutch & English, French is a plus. You are a true team player who values knowledge sharing and collective success. At SecWise , we believe that security is serious business—but work should still be fun. We’re a tight-knit team within the Cronos Group, where knowledge sharing, personal growth, and a healthy work-life balance are part of our DNA. Here’s what we offer: A competitive salary package with a company car. Great benefits through the Cronos Group care package. A flat, open culture where your ideas matter. Support to grow into the best version of yourself. Ready to engineer the future of cloud security? Apply now or reach out to learn more about how you can make an impact at SecWise.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5598365793.scraped: About the Role Connect Group is seeking an Infrastructure Engineer (Mid-Level). As an Infrastructure Engineer, you ensure a reliable, secure and high performing IT infrastructure that supports our production environment. You implement technical improvements, solve complex incidents, optimize performance and work closely with production and maintenance teams to keep operations running smoothly. Key Responsibilities Improve and maintain network and server infrastructure (WiFi upgrades, storage expansion, etc.). Resolve complex Tier2 incidents and perform root cause analysis. Execute ITILbased change management and coordinate infrastructure updates. Monitor performance and capacity of servers, storage and networks. Securely integrate factory equipment and OT networks with corporate IT. Requirements Strong experience with VMware vSphere, ESXi and vCenter. Bachelor’s degree in information technology, Computer Science, System & Network Administration, or equivalent experience. Handson knowledge of enterprise storage (Pure Storage, HPE Nimble). Skilled in Veeam Backup & Replication (backup, restore, DR testing). Solid networking skills: Fortigate, Aruba or Meraki; VLANs, VPNs, segmentation. Experience with hybrid cloud (Azure, Microsoft 365, Hybrid AD). Familiarity with monitoring tools such as PRTG. Language skills: Strong command of Dutch and English (spoken and written) to collaborate effectively with local teams and global IT stakeholders Nice to Have : ISO27001/NIS2 knowledge, DR/BCP experience, basic PowerShell scripting, understanding of MES/ERP application environments. What We Offer A key technical role in a modern manufacturing environment. Growth path toward Senior Infrastructure Engineer. Access to advanced trainings and certifications. A supportive, handson engineering culture. Competitive compensation and benefits based on experience and performance.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5591932840.scraped: Your job in a nutshell As Test Automation Engineer, you'll join an enthusiastic multidisciplinary team at our HQ in Brussels to develop qualitative applications in a fast-changing IT landscape. Your role will contribute to innovations benefiting millions of clients, ensuring product and code quality from start to finish through test automation. And in detail As a test automation engineer, you'll be joining an enthusiastic multidisciplinary team. Together, you develop qualitative applications for a fast-changing IT landscape: an exciting and versatile challenge! You monitor product and code quality, from start to finish You help determine the feature test plan and the test scope You focus maximum on automation of test cases You share your test results You identify and correct any sticking points and iron out any bugs Your talents and competences You're the physical embodiment of the Scrum values: involvement, focus, openness and respect, with a good dose of courage to boot You're inquisitive and driven—and you're Always On the ball You thrive in a team environment, happily sharing your knowledge and insights You are quick to make connections and dare to question everything that deserves to be questioned You obtained your bachelor or master degree in Computer Sciences or IT You have a good knowledge of Dutch or French, and great English skills You know your way around Develop and maintain automated test scripts using Java and test automation frameworks (e.g., Selenium, TestNG, JUnit, Cucumber etc.). Create comprehensive test plans, test cases, and test data for both functional and non-functional testing. Execute automated tests, analyze results, and report defects with detailed logs and steps for replication. Integrate test automation into CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment) pipelines. Work with the development team to identify areas for automation and prioritize test cases. Debug and troubleshoot automation scripts and applications in various environments. Ensure the scalability, reliability, and effectiveness of automated test frameworks and tools. Maintain documentation for test automation frameworks, processes, and results. Have knowledge on the standards in software testing (ISTQB) You surprise us with your experience or knowledge of the tools and/or programming languages below Java Selenium Cypress Gherkin JMeter Postman Ruby PHP Jenkins Swagger GitLab Python What we have to offer Sustainability AND impact We strongly believe in the positive impact that the financial sector can have on the world. BNP Paribas Fortis offers sustainable and responsible investment products and solutions and encourages social entrepreneurship. Together with our customers, we are working towards a better future every day. Development & career perspectives The departments that make up BNP Paribas Fortis cover a wide range of activities. You will therefore have many opportunities for "horizontal" and "vertical" career development. Good place TO work In addition to your remuneration and benefits (meal vouchers, insurance, holidays), we offer flexible salary options, allowing you to convert part of your salary into personalised benefits (phone use, IT equipment, transport, etc.). Our engagement At BNP Paribas Fortis, we want to attract and retain all talent, whatever their gender, age, background or sexual orientation, and irrespective of whether they are living with a disability, as every person has their own experiences and their own identity. All of our full-time vacancies are also open to candidates wishing to work on an 80% or 90% full-time equivalent basis. Tempted by the challenge? A first selection is made based on your CV and motivation letter. Are we convinced that you are the right candidate? Then we will contact you. Good luck! Is this position not for you, but do you know someone who is cut out for this job? Feel free to share it with friends and family! Our engagement At BNP Paribas Fortis, we want to attract and retain all talent, whatever their gender, age, background or sexual orientation, and irrespective of whether they are living with a disability, as all persons have their own experiences and their own identity. https://www.adzuna.be/details/5591637094.scraped: Description En tant que Design & Build Engineer, vos missions consisteront à : Concevoir et planifier des projets techniques en respectant les exigences clients et réglementaires ; Participer à la réalisation des études de faisabilité, des designs techniques et des plans d’exécution ; Collaborer avec les équipes de construction et les parties prenantes pour assurer le suivi des projets ; Superviser l’intégration des solutions techniques et veiller à la qualité, aux coûts et aux délais ; Contribuer à l’amélioration continue des processus de conception et de construction. Company Notre client est un acteur majeur du secteur de la construction et des concessions, reconnu pour ses projets d’envergure et son engagement envers l’innovation et la qualité. Présent à l’international, il offre un environnement dynamique où l’expertise technique et la collaboration sont valorisées, et où chaque collaborateur peut contribuer à des projets ambitieux et structurants. Profile Vous êtes le profil idéal pour cette mission de Design & Build Engineer si : Vous êtes titulaire d’un master en ingénierie civile, mécanique, électrique ou dans un domaine similaire ; Vous disposez d’une expérience significative sur des projets d’installation et/ou des projets de type DBFM ; Vous possédez de bonnes connaissances en HVAC ; Vous maîtrisez les logiciels de conception et de modélisation (ex. : AutoCAD, Revit, BIM, logiciels de simulation) ; Vous êtes rigoureux(se), autonome et capable de travailler en équipe multidisciplinaire ; Vous avez un excellent sens de l’organisation et de la communication , et savez gérer plusieurs projets simultanément . Offer Pour cette offre de Design & Build Engineer, notre client vous offre : Un package salarial compétitif et des avantages sociaux attractifs ; La possibilité de contribuer à des projets techniques innovants et d’envergure ; Un environnement de travail stimulant au sein d’un groupe reconnu à l’international ; Des opportunités de développement professionnel et de formation continue.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5590138650.scraped: Chez Anderson, nous accompagnons les PME dans la modernisation, la sécurisation et l’exploitation de leurs environnements IT. En tant qu’Ingénieur·e Système Cloud, tu joues un rôle clé dans la qualité et la fiabilité des environnements IT de nos clients. Tu travailles directement avec des PME pour exploiter, faire évoluer et sécuriser leurs environnements. Ton rôle combine expertise technique, contact client et vision long terme : résoudre des problématiques lorsqu’elles se présentent — du support quotidien L1 et L2 aux incidents complexes L3 — mais surtout construire des environnements modernes, fiables et sécurisés. Tes responsabilités Tu participes activement à la gestion quotidienne, à l’évolution et à la sécurisation des environnements IT de nos clients. Concrètement, tu es amené·e à : Gérer et maintenir les environnements Microsoft 365 et Azure Assurer leur disponibilité, leurs performances et leur sécurité Intervenir directement auprès des clients pour résoudre des incidents techniques complexes et en prévenir la récurrence Participer aux migrations vers Microsoft 365 et Azure (messagerie, identité, fichiers, postes de travail) Déployer et maintenir les fonctionnalités de sécurité Microsoft (Defender, MFA, accès conditionnel) Contribuer aux stratégies de sauvegarde, de reprise et de résilience Collaborer avec les autres ingénieur·e·s pour assurer la continuité de service Documenter les environnements, procédures et bonnes pratiques Identifier des opportunités d’amélioration pour les clients et pour Anderson Qui tu es Bachelier ou master en informatique (ou équivalent par l’expérience) 2 à 3 ans d’expérience dans des environnements systèmes et/ou cloud À l’aise dans le contact direct avec les clients À l’aise aussi bien dans le support utilisateur que dans des tâches d’ingénierie systèmes Autonome, structuré·e et responsable En veille technologique continue, avec l’envie d’apprendre Ton environnement technique Microsoft 365 (Exchange, SharePoint, Teams), Azure & Entra ID Intune et les concepts de Modern Workplace Virtualisation (Hyper-V, VMware) Sauvegarde & reprise (Veeam) Notions solides en sécurité (identité, protection des endpoints, contrôle des accès) Tu ne dois pas tout maîtriser dès le premier jour : nous privilégions l’état d’esprit, la capacité d’analyse et l’envie d’évoluer. Pourquoi rejoindre Anderson ? Un impact réel sur les clients et les solutions mises en place Des projets Cloud & Sécurité modernes Un rôle qui combine expertise technique et relation client Des perspectives d’évolution selon ton profil et tes ambitions Ce que nous offrons Package salarial compétitif Voiture de société et carte carburant Chèques-repas, éco-chèques, primes Télétravail avec indemnité forfaitaire Assurance groupe et hospitalisation (couverture familiale) GSM et abonnement Budget formation & certifications Une équipe engagée et technique

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5576255459.scraped: About Lizy LIZY is revolutionizing the €70 billion European car leasing industry, one of the last giants yet to be disrupted. Traditional leasing is slow, confusing, and overpriced. We’re changing that. At LIZY, we believe leasing a car should be as seamless as buying a toaster on Amazon. With the premium touch of a Four Seasons experience. Since launching in 2019, we've been on an unstoppable growth journey, driven by bold ambition and relentless innovation. Our vision? To become the #1 digital car leasing platform in Europe. Customers love us: over 250 raving reviews and an average rating of 4.7 stars. Headquartered in Brussels, we’ve already expanded into Paris and Amsterdam, and we’re just getting started. Backed by over €40 million in funding and powered by a dream team of top talent from Google, McKinsey, Bain, Deloitte, Cowboy, Teamleader, and more, we’re building the future of car leasing. LIZY values Fun (Have it): Enjoy the ride - life is short. Feedback (Give it): Feedback is the breakfast of champions. Always assume positive intent. Personal Growth (Master it): Never stop learning and improving. Wow (Deliver it): Actually make our customers say "WOW". ️‍♂️ Drive (Show it): Show commitment and bring everything you have. Do what you say and say what you do The Position We are looking for a Business Automation Engineer to act as the "force multiplier" for LIZY. You will sit at the intersection of Business, Operations, and Technology, using the latest no-code and AI tools to turn manual, complex processes into automated magic. You won't just be a ticket-taker, you are a builder at heart with a strong business mind. You will follow the product roadmap to deploy robust internal tools, while simultaneously acting as the "go-to expert" for business teams who are building their own smaller automations. This is a role for a tech-savvy problem solver who loves to hack processes, is obsessed with the latest AI trends, and gets a kick out of saving the company hundreds of hours of manual work. What you’ll be doing: Build & deploy : You are the primary builder for complex, cross-functional automations. You will use tools like Make, n8n, Zapier, and Python scripting to connect our systems and automate heavy operational workflows. Process architecture : Before building, you analyze. You work with business teams to deconstruct their pain points, challenge their logic, and structure data flows that make sense for the business. You’ll have the chance to interact with all functions at LIZY, having a transversal role with major impact for the organisation. Coach & unblock : You are the internal champion for automation. When Sales or Ops colleagues get stuck building their own automations, you are the expert they call for help. You review their logic and help them level up. Tech watch & innovation : The field of AI and Automation moves fast. You are responsible for staying curious, testing new tools, and bringing fresh ideas to the Product team to keep our no-code stack cutting-edge. Contribute to the roadmap : You work closely with Product to identify opportunities. You don’t just build what is asked; you provide feedback on feasibility and suggest smarter ways to achieve the goal. ‍‍♂️About you You are a builder first. You love getting your hands dirty. You prefer prototyping and shipping a working solution over writing a 10-page strategy document. You speak business and tech. You can explain API constraints to a salesperson, and you can explain a sales funnel to a developer. You have a strong business acumen and are able to empathise with different areas of an organization. You are endlessly curious. You are the person who reads about a new AI model release and immediately tries to figure out how to use it to streamline tedious repetitive tasks. You know that a bad process automated is just a faster bad process. You care about error handling, documentation, and maintainability. Your profile: 1-3 years in a role blending Ops and Tech (e.g., Business analyst, Tech consultant, Operations manager with a coding background). Experience building automation flows in Make, Zapier, n8n (or similar) is essential. Functional knowledge of Python (or JavaScript) to manipulate data and connect APIs when no-code isn't enough is a plus. You understand how databases relate (SQL concepts) and how to structure data cleanly. You have a "Process Improvement" brain. You look at a manual task and instinctively calculate how to automate it. Fluent in English. Perks and Benefits At LIZY, we know that building a winning business starts with building an incredible team. That’s why we go the extra mile to make sure our people are supported, empowered, and inspired—every single day. Here’s what you can expect when you join us: Competitive & Optimized Package: We offer an attractive compensation package that includes a well-structured salary and a lot of other benefits. Flexible Mobility Budget: Need a car? Great. Prefer other mobility options? We’ve got you covered. You choose what fits your lifestyle best. Cool Offices in the Heart of Brussels: Expect a vibrant atmosphere, awesome colleagues, specialty coffee, premium tea, and lunch paid by the company every Wednesday. ☀️ Flexible Work, Your Way: You can work remotely up to 2-3 days a week. Top-Tier Tools: We equip you with the best: high-end laptop, external monitor, iPhone, and anything else you need to do your best work. Stock Options: When LIZY wins, so do you. We believe in sharing success. A Culture That Actually Cares: Expect positivity, transparency, and support—because we believe great things happen when people feel good at work. We care about each other and we show it—through weekly team lunches, monthly All Hands meetings with cake and drinks, and fun team-building events throughout the year.

Top-Tier Tools: We equip you with the best: high-end laptop, external monitor, iPhone, and anything else you need to do your best work. Stock Options: When LIZY wins, so do you. We believe in sharing success. A Culture That Actually Cares: Expect positivity, transparency, and support—because we believe great things happen when people feel good at work. We care about each other and we show it—through weekly team lunches, monthly All Hands meetings with cake and drinks, and fun team-building events throughout the year.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5576255459.scraped: About Lizy LIZY is revolutionizing the €70 billion European car leasing industry, one of the last giants yet to be disrupted. Traditional leasing is slow, confusing, and overpriced. We’re changing that. At LIZY, we believe leasing a car should be as seamless as buying a toaster on Amazon. With the premium touch of a Four Seasons experience. Since launching in 2019, we've been on an unstoppable growth journey, driven by bold ambition and relentless innovation. Our vision? To become the #1 digital car leasing platform in Europe. Customers love us: over 250 raving reviews and an average rating of 4.7 stars. Headquartered in Brussels, we’ve already expanded into Paris and Amsterdam, and we’re just getting started. Backed by over €40 million in funding and powered by a dream team of top talent from Google, McKinsey, Bain, Deloitte, Cowboy, Teamleader, and more, we’re building the future of car leasing. LIZY values Fun (Have it): Enjoy the ride - life is short. Feedback (Give it): Feedback is the breakfast of champions. Always assume positive intent. Personal Growth (Master it): Never stop learning and improving. Wow (Deliver it): Actually make our customers say "WOW". ️‍♂️ Drive (Show it): Show commitment and bring everything you have. Do what you say and say what you do The Position We are looking for a Business Automation Engineer to act as the "force multiplier" for LIZY. You will sit at the intersection of Business, Operations, and Technology, using the latest no-code and AI tools to turn manual, complex processes into automated magic. You won't just be a ticket-taker, you are a builder at heart with a strong business mind. You will follow the product roadmap to deploy robust internal tools, while simultaneously acting as the "go-to expert" for business teams who are building their own smaller automations. This is a role for a tech-savvy problem solver who loves to hack processes, is obsessed with the latest AI trends, and gets a kick out of saving the company hundreds of hours of manual work. What you’ll be doing: Build & deploy : You are the primary builder for complex, cross-functional automations. You will use tools like Make, n8n, Zapier, and Python scripting to connect our systems and automate heavy operational workflows. Process architecture : Before building, you analyze. You work with business teams to deconstruct their pain points, challenge their logic, and structure data flows that make sense for the business. You’ll have the chance to interact with all functions at LIZY, having a transversal role with major impact for the organisation. Coach & unblock : You are the internal champion for automation. When Sales or Ops colleagues get stuck building their own automations, you are the expert they call for help. You review their logic and help them level up. Tech watch & innovation : The field of AI and Automation moves fast. You are responsible for staying curious, testing new tools, and bringing fresh ideas to the Product team to keep our no-code stack cutting-edge. Contribute to the roadmap : You work closely with Product to identify opportunities. You don’t just build what is asked; you provide feedback on feasibility and suggest smarter ways to achieve the goal. ‍‍♂️About you You are a builder first. You love getting your hands dirty. You prefer prototyping and shipping a working solution over writing a 10-page strategy document. You speak business and tech. You can explain API constraints to a salesperson, and you can explain a sales funnel to a developer. You have a strong business acumen and are able to empathise with different areas of an organization. You are endlessly curious. You are the person who reads about a new AI model release and immediately tries to figure out how to use it to streamline tedious repetitive tasks. You know that a bad process automated is just a faster bad process. You care about error handling, documentation, and maintainability. Your profile: 1-3 years in a role blending Ops and Tech (e.g., Business analyst, Tech consultant, Operations manager with a coding background). Experience building automation flows in Make, Zapier, n8n (or similar) is essential. Functional knowledge of Python (or JavaScript) to manipulate data and connect APIs when no-code isn't enough is a plus. You understand how databases relate (SQL concepts) and how to structure data cleanly. You have a "Process Improvement" brain. You look at a manual task and instinctively calculate how to automate it. Fluent in English. Perks and Benefits At LIZY, we know that building a winning business starts with building an incredible team. That’s why we go the extra mile to make sure our people are supported, empowered, and inspired—every single day. Here’s what you can expect when you join us: Competitive & Optimized Package: We offer an attractive compensation package that includes a well-structured salary and a lot of other benefits. Flexible Mobility Budget: Need a car? Great. Prefer other mobility options? We’ve got you covered. You choose what fits your lifestyle best. Cool Offices in the Heart of Brussels: Expect a vibrant atmosphere, awesome colleagues, specialty coffee, premium tea, and lunch paid by the company every Wednesday. ☀️ Flexible Work, Your Way: You can work remotely up to 2-3 days a week. Top-Tier Tools: We equip you with the best: high-end laptop, external monitor, iPhone, and anything else you need to do your best work. Stock Options: When LIZY wins, so do you. We believe in sharing success. A Culture That Actually Cares: Expect positivity, transparency, and support—because we believe great things happen when people feel good at work. We care about each other and we show it—through weekly team lunches, monthly All Hands meetings with cake and drinks, and fun team-building events throughout the year.

active:bg-adzuna-green-700 text-center block m-4 md:mx-0 md:my-4 md:inline-block " Solliciteren /a section class="adp-body mx-4 mb-4 text-sm md:mx-0 md:text-base md:mb-0" Actemium Belgium conseille, conçoit, réalise et soutient des processus industriels. Nous offrons également du service et des formations. Nous sommes actifs en électricité et instrumentation, en informatique / TIC, en automatisation et en mécanique et tuyauterie. Notre mission est d'aider nos clients à améliorer leur avantage concurrentiel ainsi que leur performance industrielle. Actemium Belgium emploie aujourd'hui 900 personnes, réparties dans 8 sites en Belgique, 1 en Chine et 1 en France. Actemium est une marque de VINCI Energies. Actemium, marque de VINCI Energies Belgium, entièrement dédiée à l'Industrie est aussi leader dans la fourniture de solutions et de services à l'Industrie. Notre focus, l'amélioration des processus industriels chez nos clients. Dans le cadre de notre croissance en Wallonie, nous recherchons un Automation Engineer avec une solide expérience en processus industriels et un esprit d'équipe fort. Au sein d'une équipe projets, vous travaillez de manière autonome à des solutions sur mesure chez nos clients actifs en Wallonie et issus exclusivement du secteur industriel. Responsabiités Programmer et implémenter des systèmes PLC, SCADA ou DCS chez nos clients Intégrer et paramétrer des systèmes IT industriels Analyser les process et réfléchir, le cas échéant, à la mise au point de nouveaux concepts Réaliser des analyses fonctionnelles adaptées aux situations techniques rencontrées Implémenter les fonctionnalités et réaliser les mises en service chez nos clients Rédiger la documentation technique de la solution Supporter/exécuter les processus de validation du système Profil Master en Sciences de l'Ingénieur Industriel, Master/Bachelor Automation Idéalement 5 ans d'expérience en automatisation industrielle, une expérience dans l'industrie de process constitue un réel atout Connaissance en automates programmables Siemens (S7, TIA, PCS7) Connaissance en systèmes de supervision SCADA (WinCC, InTouch, Aveva System Platform) constitue un sérieux atout Esprit analytoque fort et rigueur (respect des délais et des normes de sécurité) Flexible et curieux, vous adoptez une attitude « problem-solving » en toute circonstance Francophone, vous êtes à l'aise en anglais Notre offre Actemium fait partie du groupe VINCI Energies. Chez nous, l'accent est mis sur : Des projets innovants L'Esprit d'équipe, l'implication dans l'organisation et le développement personnel Une formation sur mesure pour encadrer de manière optimale vos débuts chez nous Un parcours de carrière clair ... conçoit, réalise et soutient des processus industriels. Nous offrons également du service et des formations. Nous sommes actifs en électricité et instrumentation, en informatique / TIC, en automatisation et en mécanique et tuyauterie. Notre mission est d'aider nos clients à améliorer leur avantage concurrentiel ainsi que leur performance industrielle. Actemium Belgium emploie aujourd'hui 900 personnes, réparties dans 8 sites en Belgique, 1 en Chine et 1 en France. Actemium est une marque de VINCI Energies. Actemium, marque de VINCI Energies Belgium, entièrement dédiée à l'Industrie est aussi leader dans la fourniture de solutions et de services à l'Industrie. Notre focus, l'amélioration des processus industriels chez nos clients. Dans le cadre de notre croissance en Wallonie, nous recherchons un Automation Engineer avec une solide expérience en processus industriels et un esprit d'équipe fort. Au sein d'une équipe projets, vous travaillez de manière autonome à des solutions sur mesure chez nos clients actifs en Wallonie et issus exclusivement du secteur industriel. Responsabiités Programmer et implémenter des systèmes PLC, SCADA ou DCS chez nos clients Intégrer et paramétrer des systèmes IT industriels Analyser les process et réfléchir, le cas échéant, à la mise au point de nouveaux concepts Réaliser des analyses fonctionnelles adaptées aux situations techniques rencontrées Implémenter les fonctionnalités et réaliser les mises en service chez nos clients Rédiger la documentation technique de la solution Supporter/exécuter les processus de validation du système Profil Master en Sciences de l'Ingénieur Industriel, Master/Bachelor Automation Idéalement 5 ans d'expérience en automatisation industrielle, une expérience dans l'industrie de process constitue un réel atout Connaissance en automates programmables Siemens (S7, TIA, PCS7) Connaissance en systèmes de supervision SCADA (WinCC, InTouch, Aveva System Platform) constitue un sérieux atout Esprit analytoque fort et rigueur (respect des délais et des normes de sécurité) Flexible et curieux, vous adoptez une attitude « problem-solving » en toute circonstance Francophone, vous êtes à l'aise en anglais Notre offre Actemium fait partie du groupe VINCI Energies. Chez nous, l'accent est mis sur : Des projets innovants L'Esprit d'équipe, l'implication dans l'organisation et le développement personnel Une formation sur mesure pour encadrer de manière optimale vos débuts chez nous Un parcours de carrière clair ... Dans le cadre de notre croissance en Wallonie, nous recherchons un Automation Engineer avec une solide expérience en processus industriels et un esprit d'équipe fort. Au sein d'une équipe projets, vous travaillez de manière autonome à des solutions sur mesure chez nos clients actifs en Wallonie et issus exclusivement du secteur industriel. Responsabiités Programmer et implémenter des systèmes PLC, SCADA ou DCS chez nos clients Intégrer et paramétrer des systèmes IT industriels Analyser les process et réfléchir, le cas échéant, à la mise au point de nouveaux concepts Réaliser des analyses fonctionnelles adaptées aux situations techniques rencontrées Implémenter les fonctionnalités et réaliser les mises en service chez nos clients Rédiger la documentation technique de la solution Supporter/exécuter les processus de validation du système Profil Master en Sciences de l'Ingénieur Industriel, Master/Bachelor Automation Idéalement 5 ans d'expérience en automatisation industrielle, une expérience dans l'industrie de process constitue un réel atout Connaissance en automates programmables Siemens (S7, TIA, PCS7) Connaissance en systèmes de supervision SCADA (WinCC, InTouch, Aveva System Platform) constitue un sérieux atout Esprit analytoque fort et rigueur (respect des délais et des normes de sécurité) Flexible et curieux, vous adoptez une attitude « problem-solving » en toute circonstance Francophone, vous êtes à l'aise en anglais Notre offre Actemium fait partie du groupe VINCI Energies. Chez nous, l'accent est mis sur : Des projets innovants L'Esprit d'équipe, l'implication dans l'organisation et le développement personnel Une formation sur mesure pour encadrer de manière optimale vos débuts chez nous Un parcours de carrière clair ... https://www.adzuna.be/details/5630187829.scraped: Luminus is looking for the next generation of climate changers Join our two-year traineeship and make an active contribution to a CO2-neutral energy future and the energy transition. As one of our 10 young graduates, you choose ... your projects to make a difference. The Luminus vibe. Can you feel it? As a Luminus trainee you don’t have to be an expert in a specific domain. What matters is your energy, your drive and your eagerness to learn! So, are you a positive person, an entrepreneur and a team player? Are you open-minded, curious and is taking care of the environment important to you? Great! It seems like we match! Luminus values e.v.e.r.y individual. Are you looking for a workplace where you can truly be yourself, where you feel respected, supported regardless of your background, identity and unique story? Then you’ve come to the right place. Diversity equity and inclusion are not empty words for us — we grant that everyone can flourish and feel valued, so let’s build it together! What does that mean in practice? Do you have specific needs or require adjustments during the recruitment process or once you are hired? Let’s discuss about it and we’ll do our best to find a solution. We can also discuss if you want to work at 80% or 100%. Together we make the difference. #Electrify2030 Do you recognise yourself in this? I graduated with a master's degree. I am fluent in Dutch and/or French and English. I am a starter or don’t have more than 2 years of work experience. I am a born entrepreneur, and love nothing more than to take the initiative and learn. I breathe the 3 Luminus corporate values: entrepreneurship, all together and customer first. I share the green ambitions of Luminus. I am available on 26.03.2026 to join Zero Camp, the last step in the selection process. Check out Zero Camp and what other young graduates say about Generation Zero. The traineeship in a nutshell A 2-year trainee programme starting in September 2026, with 3 completely different 8-month assignments in different departments/teams, allowing you to learn and grow. Your traineeship will take shape as we align our business challenges with your skills, interests, competencies, and knowledge. Flexibility is key! Dive into challenges, develop essential skills and build an invaluable network within & outside Luminus. After two years, you will choose a job that energises you and matches your developed expertise, interests and values. Check out this list of advantages for you. Grab this unique opportunity and grow in an ever-changing and therefore challenging energysector. Develop a range of new skills and gain valuable expertise in various forward-looking areas. Help complete various Luminus projects. Co-determine their direction and be proud of your realisations. Build a strong network on which you can count in the future. Or that can count on you And last but not least… A permanent contract with an attractive salary, an electric company car or mobility budget, a bonus and a range of interesting additional benefits. Check out Zero Camp and what other young graduates say about Generation Zero. Luminus is leading the energy transition in Belgium and is active in electricity generation, energy supply, energy solutions, and flexibility services. Did you know that… we are number one in onshore wind energy and hydropower. our flexible production units help keep the grid stable and reliable. we supply energy to nearly 24% of households and businesses. we help customers electrify to reduce their CO₂ emissions while keeping their budgets under control. we are investing heavily in energy storage and flexibility, ensuring renewable energy is always available when needed. more than 3,000 Luminus colleagues work every day toward a CO₂-neutral future and the energy transition. In this way, we actively contribute to protecting the planet, improving people’s well-being, and driving economic progress. Make the switch to Luminus today!
