https://www.adzuna.be/details/5608565375.scraped: Our partner is a leading international financial firm with a strong presence across the European market. As the business continues its rapid growth, they are seeking a motivated ICT Administrator to work closely with the IT Manager and play a key role in managing and evolving the IT infrastructure. Tired of working in isolation, without support or recognition? This is the opportunity that will change your career ICT Administrator - What's the job about Provide on-site and remote support for hardware, software, and network issues , ensuring seamless operation of laptops, printers, phones, and other peripherals. Install, configure, and maintain operating systems, applications, and IT infrastructure , including servers, virtual environments, and cloud services. Manage IT security, compliance, and documentation while promoting best practices and user awareness . Assist with equipment deployment, lifecycle management , and end-user training to optimize technology adoption. Collaborate with the ICT manager on system improvements and deliver exceptional technical support across all company offices. ICT Administrator - Who are you Minimum 2–3 years of hands-on experience in technical roles. Bachelor or Master degree in IT (mandatory) Proactive and solution-oriented , eager to learn and adopt new technologies . Resourceful with a “Swiss knife” mindset , able to handle diverse challenges effectively. Responsible, motivated, and customer-focused , delivering high-quality results. Excellent team player with the ability to work independently , fluent in English and French or Dutch . ICT Administrator - What are the advantages Contribute to IT initiatives that support a growing organization. Collaborate with skilled colleagues in a fastpaced environment. Develop your technical expertise in a role that offers autonomy. Work in the IT field across multiple European offices. Attractive salary with access to a well-equipped fitness center .

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5614685551.scraped: Volvo Group Operations Graduate Program Over 15 exciting months, the operations graduate program will give you a comprehensive view of the Volvo Group’s global truck manufacturing organization. You’ll explore our brands, plants, logistics, and key business areas like manufacturing, quality, and continuous improvement. You'll also engage with emerging technologies and develop future-ready skills. From day one of the traineeship, you’ll hold a permanent position, supported by a manager and mentor, with opportunities for “on-the-job” rotations and real project experience. Through team development sessions and learning modules, you'll connect with other participants and grow into your future career at Volvo. If you're seeking a traineeship with global exposure and the chance to shape the future, this role could be your next step. Our organization You will become part of a dynamic community of production, tooling, maintenance, system and technology engineers — the true technical heartbeat of our Volvo Truck assembly plant. Together, we design, analyze and continuously refine the way our products come to life. Innovation, collaboration and improvement are at the core of everything we do. Working side by side with a diverse, cross-functional network of experts from operations, safety, quality and other engineering teams, you will help shape smart, future-proof solutions for a complex, large-scale production environment. From introducing new products and rebalancing processes to integrating cutting-edge technologies and driving purposeful digitalization. No two challenges are the same. If you’re eager to learn, make an impact, and grow as an engineer — this is where your journey starts. What will you do? As a Digitalization Engineer, you are at the forefront of transforming how our factories think, operate, and evolve. Your mission is to accelerate the digital transformation of our industrial and engineering ecosystem by turning real-world manufacturing challenges into innovative, scalable digital solutions. You bridge engineering excellence with data, automation, and cutting-edge digital technologies to enhance efficiency, quality, safety, and sustainability across our truck manufacturing network. This is where engineering meets innovation. Where ideas become reality. And where you don’t just adapt to the future of manufacturing — you help create it. As a data-minded engineer, you leverage data to uncover high-impact opportunities where digital tools and automation deliver measurable value, fully aligned with our business strategy and goals. You spot, explore and interpret trends and patterns emerging from data analyses or programming, empowering data-driven decision-making across the organization. You support the implementation of industrial tools and advanced reporting, making insights accessible to a broad user community. You help define standards and analytical frameworks, contributing to the continuous optimization of processes through close cross-functional collaboration. You work efficiently, securely and ethically, using emerging technologies validated by Volvo Group and fully aligned with its cybersecurity standards and core values. Who are you? You hold (or will graduate with) a bachelor's or master's degree in a technical field such as Electromechanics, Automation or IT. Certifications in IoT or cloud platforms and big data tools are a strong plus. Maximum two years of previous full-time working experience, prior or after graduation. You have a genuine interest in end-to-end business processes and help translate business needs into clear requirements. You collaborate closely with professionals to design, build and roll out scalable, sustainable solutions. You are a hands-on, practical analyst with strong organizational and planning skills, naturally taking ownership and initiative in your work. You can communicate insights clearly, presenting findings to stakeholders through compelling visualizations and actionable recommendations. As this is a truly global program, you need to be fluent in both speaking and writing in English. A very good knowledge of Dutch or willingness to learn Dutch is mandatory. Ready for the next move? If you're seeking global exposure and the chance to shape the future, this role could be your next step. Curious? Apply now! Please send us your application before the 11th of March 2026. We will not accept new applications after this. Start of the traineeship will be mid August 2026 Volvo Group is one of the leading manufacturers of trucks, buses, earth-moving machinery, engines and powertrains for recreational and commercial vessels, as well as industrial applications. The Ghent facility is the Volvo Group's largest truck factory in the world. The Ghent site is also home to several logistics activities, including the group's largest distribution center. In addition, you will also find a number of European services such as assistance and breakdown assistance for drivers (Volvo Action Service) and technical support for dealers (Technical Dealer Support). As a newcomer at Volvo Group, you’ll have the opportunity to work in a dynamic, international environment where you’ll apply the latest technologies in the transport and logistics sector. You will have the chance to grow, use your talents, and shape your future in the sector. And you can try our famous Swedish meatballs in the cafeteria! Check out our vacancies!

As a newcomer at Volvo Group, you’ll have the opportunity to work in a dynamic, international environment where you’ll apply the latest technologies in the transport and logistics sector. You will have the chance to grow, use your talents, and shape your future in the sector. And you can try our famous Swedish meatballs in the cafeteria! Check out our vacancies!

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5614685611.scraped: Volvo Group Operations Program - Traineeship Over 15 exciting months, the operations graduate program will give you a comprehensive view of the Volvo Group’s global truck manufacturing organization. You’ll explore our brands, plants, logistics, and key business areas like manufacturing, quality, and continuous improvement. You'll also engage with emerging technologies and develop future-ready skills. From day one of the traineeship, you’ll hold a permanent position, supported by a manager and mentor, with opportunities for “on-the-job” rotations and real project experience. Through team development sessions and learning modules, you'll connect with other participants and grow into your future career at Volvo. If you're seeking a traineeship with global exposure and the chance to shape the future, this role could be your next step. Our organization Service Operations & technology (SO&T) is one of the key departments within Volvo Group, and our Ghent Distribution Center is the largest Distribution Center of the Volvo Group worldwide. It holds spare parts for multiple brands of the Volvo Group, including Volvo Trucks, Buses, Construction Equipment, and our marine- and industrial engines. Next to storage, our mission is to drive delivery precision, efficiency and service excellence across the globe. This is us, your new team You'll be joining a diverse, cross-functional team of operators, engineers, analysts, and operations experts who work both globally and locally to drive the future of our distribution network. From day one, you’ll be in the driver's seat of meaningful initiatives—combining a long-term strategic vision with hands-on, day-to-day impact. Your role as Future Leader at Volvo Group From day one, you will hold a permanent position as a member of the local leadership team in our Distribution Center in Ghent. Your journey starts by working alongside different operations teams, to truly understand the day-to-day reality and our logistics flow. By engaging in on-the-job rotations, you will gain valuable insight into domains such as continuous improvement, quality, digitalization, and logistics engineering. To support your development, you’ll receive leadership training and mentoring from experienced Volvo leaders, and participate in group sessions designed to strengthen both your personal and professional skills. You will deep dive into critical areas of our business, while gaining a broader understanding of our end-to-end supply chain, aftermarket services, and remanufacturing operations. Throughout the program, you’ll contribute to our digital transformation, exploring and applying emerging technologies to help connect and automate processes across the organization. Think AI, data science, machine learning, circular systems, and virtual manufacturing—essential tools in building our fully electric and autonomous products of the future. How you will make an impact You ensure daily operations run smoothly, you manage a team of blue collars and support their development You lead and inspire your team, ensuring safety, quality, efficiency, and performance. You drive engagement through positive team communication and individual coaching. You tackle operational challenges hands-on and implement improvements. You collaborate with cross-functional teams on larger logistics projects and transformation initiatives. You grow your leadership maturity through experience, feedback and reflection. This role provides the perfect foundation for a long-term leadership career, blending strategic vision with real-world experience on the floor. Are you our new Leader? You hold a Master’s degree in Engineering, Science, Economics or equivalent You have a strong interest in logistics and digitalization You have clear ambition to grow into a leadership or management role You are someone who wants to lead people as much as you want to optimize processes You have a natural sense of ownership, strong communication skills, and a healthy dose of empathy As this is a truly global program, you need to be fluent in both speaking and writing in English. A very good knowledge of Dutch is mandatory. You hold a valid work permit for Belgium. Maximum two years of previous full-time working experience, prior to or after graduation. Are we the perfect match? If this is you, please jump in and join us for the ride! Please send us your application before the 11th of March 2026. We will not accept new applications after this. The start of the traineeship will be mid August 2026. https://www.adzuna.be/details/5619875118.scraped: Project Overview The primary goal of this internship is to transform our internal documentation into a high-functioning, visually engaging knowledge hub. Using a pre-defined structural proposition, you will lead the migration of existing knowledge, including the Atrias Academy , into Confluence. This isn't just about "copy-pasting"; it’s about creating a seamless user experience for every employee who needs to find information. Key Responsibilities System Implementation: Execute the migration of paper-based structures into a live, organized Confluence environment. Content Transformation: Review legacy documentation and rewrite/reformat it to ensure it is concise, accurate, and easy to navigate. Visual Design: Utilize Confluence macros, layouts, and visual elements to make pages "scannable" and aesthetically pleasing. Academy Integration: Transition the Atrias Academy materials into the new structure, ensuring learning paths are intuitive for new and existing staff. Quality Assurance: Audit links, permissions, and page hierarchies to ensure the "new world" of our documentation is bug-free and logically sound. Required Skills & Qualifications Category Desired Skillset Technical Familiarity with Confluence (or similar wikis/CMS) and an understanding of Information Architecture . Communication Strong technical writing skills; the ability to take complex info and make it "human-readable." Design Eye A knack for UI/UX, knowing how to use white space, headings, and imagery to prevent "walls of text." Mindset High attention to detail and a structured, methodical approach to problem-solving. What’s in it for the Intern? Ownership: They won't just be a "task-runner"; they will be responsible for a tangible, company-wide tool. Portfolio Building: They can demonstrate a clear "Before vs. After" transformation of a corporate knowledge base. Strategic Impact: Their work will directly improve the onboarding speed and daily efficiency of the entire team. About Accenture Accenture is a leading global professional services company that helps the world’s leading businesses, governments and other organizations build their digital core, optimize their operations, accelerate revenue growth and enhance citizen services—creating tangible value at speed and scale. We are a talent- and innovation-led company with approximately 791,000 people serving clients in more than 120 countries. Technology is at the core of change today, and we are one of the world’s leaders in helping drive that change, with strong ecosystem relationships. We combine our strength in technology and leadership in cloud, data and AI with unmatched industry experience, functional expertise and global delivery capability. Our broad range of services, solutions and assets across Strategy & Consulting, Technology, Operations, Industry X and Song, together with our culture of shared success and commitment to creating 360° value, enable us to help our clients reinvent and build trusted, lasting relationships. We measure our success by the 360° value we create for our clients, each other, our shareholders, partners and communities. Visit us at www.accenture.com Equal Employment Opportunity Statement We believe that no one should be discriminated against because of their differences. All employment decisions shall be made without regard to age, race, creed, color, religion, sex, national origin, ancestry, disability status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital status, citizenship status or any other basis as protected by applicable law. Our rich diversity makes us more innovative, more competitive, and more creative, which helps us better serve our clients and our communities.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5630323956.scraped: Joining Collibra’s Cloud Operations Team We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced Production Release Engineer to join our dynamic team. In this pivotal role, you will be instrumental in ensuring the seamless, reliable, and efficient delivery of our cutting-edge cloud services. You will act as a bridge between development, operations, and business stakeholders, driving continuous improvement, fostering innovation, and actively contributing to the architectural integrity and scalability of our platforms. This position is remote. DevOps Engineers at Collibra are responsible for Proactively research and integrate emerging technologies and cutting-edge software practices into our release processes and infrastructure. Formulate and champion new ideas and features to continuously enhance our infrastructure, functionality, and overall product effectiveness. Apply data modeling and predictive analysis techniques to anticipate and mitigate potential issues before they impact production. Act as a key liaison and facilitator between Production Engineering, other technical functions, external vendors, business partners, and end-users. Lead and actively participate in cross-team engineering discussions to design and evolve scalable, measurable, fault-tolerant, and cost-effective cloud services. Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure a holistic approach to identifying, addressing, and resolving platform and functionality issues. Actively participate in architectural design reviews and discussions, providing valuable input on features, service improvements, and new service implementations. Utilize visual tools, such as flowcharts and diagrams, effectively during design and problem-solving processes. Provide high-level support and expertise for team and cross-functional projects related to release automation and infrastructure. Maintain clear and complete understanding of complex situations through intent listening and incisive questioning to ensure accurate interpretation and resolution. You Have 5 years of experience in Release Engineering, DevOps, or a similar role focused on software delivery and infrastructure. Proven track record of driving process improvements and implementing automation in complex environments. Experience with cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP) and their respective services. Strong proficiency in CI/CD pipelines and tools (e.g., Jenkins, GitLab CI, Azure DevOps, ArgoCD). Expertise in scripting and automation languages (e.g., Python, Go, Bash). Familiarity with containerization (Docker) and orchestration (Kubernetes). Understanding of infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) principles and tools (e.g., Terraform, CloudFormation). Experience with monitoring, logging, and observability tools. Professional experience installing, configuring, managing, and/or administering ElasticSearch. You Are Exceptional with communication and interpersonal skills and have the ability to articulate complex technical concepts clearly to diverse audiences. Strong in analytical and problem-solving abilities, with a proactive and preventative mindset. Able to influence and lead discussions across engineering teams. Self-driven and takes initiative to identify and implement improvements. Measures of Success Within your first month, you will gain a deep understanding of existing release pipelines and infrastructure, proactively identifying initial pain points and potential areas for improvement. Within your third month, you will establish strong working relationships and trust with key stakeholders across development, product, and operations teams. Within your sixth month, you will contribute to a quantifiable improvement in at least one key DORA metric (e.g., Change Failure Rate reduction, Deployment Lead Time improvement) within a critical release pipeline. You will successfully implement or significantly contribute to new automation efforts that demonstrably streamline a specific aspect of the release process.

Self-driven and takes initiative to identify and implement improvements. Measures of Success Within your first month, you will gain a deep understanding of existing release pipelines and infrastructure, proactively identifying initial pain points and potential areas for improvement. Within your third month, you will establish strong working relationships and trust with key stakeholders across development, product, and operations teams. Within your sixth month, you will contribute to a quantifiable improvement in at least one key DORA metric (e.g., Change Failure Rate reduction, Deployment Lead Time improvement) within a critical release pipeline. You will successfully implement or significantly contribute to new automation efforts that demonstrably streamline a specific aspect of the release process.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5628780919.scraped: Cloud engineering Service We ARE Renewables is looking for a Cloud Engineer for one of our clients in the energy sector. Task and Responsibilities Cloud Infrastructure Management Operate, monitor, and troubleshoot managed and self-hosted platforms on Azure, including Kubernetes, Kafka, PostgreSQL, Keycloak, and Opensearch Apply patching and routine updates in line with organizational and security processes. Configure and manage access controls and secrets (e.g., with Keycloak and platform-native tools) Maintain configuration management (e.g., with tools like Terraform, Helm) Produce and update technical and operational documentation for assigned infrastructure components DevOps & Automation Support deployment activities and maintain CI/CD pipelines, leveraging Azure DevOps or similar tools Monitoring & Optimization Monitor system health and respond to platform incidents, escalating complex issues to senior staff or specialists when required Provide input to capacity and performance monitoring, reporting detected bottlenecks or improvement opportunities Support & maintenance Perform routine backups, restore operations, and basic disaster recovery activities as per documented processes Collaborate with development/engineering, security, and other teams to resolve system-level issues and support platform changes Collaboration & Support Work closely with the team to develop and deploy cloud components and infrastructure as defined in the product backlog and user stories, ensuring technical tasks are completed efficiently and to quality standards Take part in Scrum ceremonies such as sprint planning, daily stand-ups, sprint reviews, and retrospectives to share progress, address obstacles, and align work with team goals Work collaboratively with team members, contribute to ongoing refinement of processes and solutions based on agile feedback, and assist with troubleshooting and optimizing cloud services throughout the iterative development cycle Key Requirements Hands-on experience in cloud infrastructure operations with Azure, including resource provisioning, monitoring, and cost awareness Azure Access Control Azure Virtual Network Logic App Azure Firewall Azure VPN Gateway Azure Monitoring Operational skills in deploying, configuring, and supporting Kubernetes, Kafka, PostgreSQL, CEPH, Opensearch, Authentication and Authorization incl. Keycloak Familiarity with infrastructure as code and container orchestration tools (Terraform) Basic scripting and automation (e.g., PowerShell, Bash, Python) for operational tasks Experience working with CI/CD pipelines and version control (e.g., Azure DevOps, Git, ArgoCD, Helm) Solid understanding of cloud security, identity and access management, and data protection Location: Brussels + Hybrid Duration: March 2026 – December 2026

https://www.adzuna.ch/details/5567547063.scraped: Stellenbeschreibung Unser Kunde, ein renommiertes Telekommunikationsunternehmen mit Sitz in Bern, sucht einen DevOps Engineer DevSecOps . Remote-Arbeit nur innerhalb der SchweizAufgaben: Mit der bevorstehenden Betriebsaufnahme der Digitalen Plattform liegt der Fokus auf dem sicheren, stabilen Betrieb der implementierten Komponenten und Produkte. https://www.adzuna.be/details/5588904814.scraped: Toyota Motor Europe offers an 18-month Graduate Program in warehouse operations at its Parts Centre in Diest, Belgium. The key task first is to join a project called warehouse reborn (very large transformation project to improve Safety, Ergonomics, Quality, Delivery, Efficiency & Members). Also, the role combines training and hands-on experience, leading to a supervisor position managing a team of around 30 members. Ideal candidates have a background in engineering, logistics, or automotive, are fluent in Dutch and English, and show strong leadership and problem-solving skills. TOYOTA is one of the world’s largest automobile manufacturers and a leading global corporation. Founded in 1937. Toyota now sells vehicles in 170 countries and employs over 350.000 people. Based in Brussels, Belgium, and staffed by 2.700 people and more than 60 nationalities, Toyota Motor Europe (TME) handles the wholesale marketing of Toyota and Lexus vehicles, parts & accessories, and manages Toyota’s European R&D, manufacturing, and engineering operations. We are excited to be transforming into a mobility company focusing on bringing even more happiness to our customers through various mobility solutions. Team description Parts Supply Chain Group mission is to deliver the right product (service parts, accessories and information) at the right time, through lean integrated logistics, exceeding Customer & Stakeholder expectation, with engaged members realising a sustainable future. Warehouse Reborn is a large-scale transformation project where the whole warehouse operation is transformed to support future business growth and challenges. The focus points are to increase our members safety, ergonomics, as well as the efficiency of the flows and the density of the storage thanks to innovative ideas and products. You will first join Warehouse Reborn project (18 months minimum) before transferring into Warehouse Supervisor role (shift pattern). Your responsibilities Warehouse Reborn project leader: Think ahead: develop the strategy (goals, stakeholders, planning…) Convince and collaborate to reach consensus Implement the project with ownership Follow up and communicate your deliverables Reflect on the learnings and share with the organisation Warehouse supervisor: Lead and develop 4 team leaders and 32 members Performance reporting Ensure your area has the right resources to perform the tasks Implementation and follow up standardised work Solve problem with Toyota methodology For TME Graduate positions we are targeting recent University Graduates (less than 18 months of experience excluding internships) You are fluent in English (TME’s business language) and dutch You are a career starter (less than 18 months of experience excluding internships) You have a bachelor / master degree in supply chain, logistics, engineering Your values are in line with the Toyota way You have experience in project management You are analytical and can visualise data You are structured, logical and rational You are a team player with excellent communication & collaboration skills You take initiative and ownership You want to lead and manage a team in an operational environment You have a desire to learn, grow and develop your skills & competences You master Excel, Powerpoint, and other Microsoft 365 tools We offer a dynamic multicultural work environment with broad opportunities to learn every day and exciting career pathways that help you explore different disciplines or areas of expertise. Your benefits: Permanent contract and a structured 18-month Graduate Development Programme Competitive salary package based on your experience, including a bonus plan depending on individual and/or company results; Flexible reward plan that will allow you to personalise part of your reward plan and balance it in line with your priorities by opting for extra insurance, more mobility options (bike lease), extra holidays and more; Meal and eco vouchers; Insurance package (medical, life, disability, pension); Extra paid holidays (Toyota days, seniority days…) above the legal holidays. More can be added through our flexible reward package; Flexible working hours and hybrid working (homeworking, 20 days from abroad); Interesting car lease formulas and discount when buying a Toyota or Lexus; Various company discounts – Exceptional terms and prices on products and services from your favourite brands and retailers; Company restaurant – Warm dishes, salads and desserts daily, including Japanese and traditional Belgian specialties, as well as a vegan/vegetarian option; Sport facilities – Tennis, squash, volleyball, football and softball pitches are available at various Toyota sites; Team building activities and other personnel related events; Programs to support your physical and social well-being. At Toyota Motor Europe, we are committed to providing equal employment opportunity to all. All our recruitment decisions are based on the individual and their qualifications along with the job requirements. The selection process is without regard to nationality, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, religion, ethnicity, disability or other personal characteristics. We do not tolerate harassment or discrimination as our individual experiences and overall diversity is our key strength. Together we have the power to make a positive change and go beyond the ordinary. If you need any specific consideration for the interview process due to disability or any other reason, please let the recruiter know.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5623360635.scraped: As a DevSecOps Engineer within a major player in the automotive industry, you will play a key role in designing, implementing, and maintaining secure CI/CD platforms and infrastructure automation solutions. You will collaborate closely with development, security, and operations teams to ensure software delivery pipelines are efficient, scalable, and aligned with security best practices. Your expertise will directly contribute to supporting a rapidly evolving digital ecosystem. Responsibilities Design, develop, and maintain CI/CD pipelines integrating security controls (DevSecOps approach). Automate security checks and compliance requirements within build and deployment processes. Work with security teams to incorporate vulnerability scanning tools and remediation workflows. Optimize the performance, reliability, and scalability of build and deployment platforms. Support and secure cloud and on-prem environments with proper configurations. Provide technical guidance, best practices, and support to development teams. Profile / Requirements : Strong experience with CI/CD tools (Jenkins, GitLab CI, Azure DevOps, etc.). https://www.adzuna.be/details/5581422151.scraped: Bruxelles/Wallonie * Fulltime * Bachelier ou expérience équivalente Au sein de l’organisation, vous rapportez en tant que IT System administrator au IT Manager. Vos responsabilités principales En tant que System Administrator, vous veillez à maintenir les systèmes IT de Matexi sains, stables et adaptés aux besoins de l’organisation. Vos compétences en résolution de problèmes sont mises à l’épreuve lorsque vous abordez de manière proactive des défis techniques. Vous agissez comme partenaire du business en vous plongeant dans le fonctionnement de l’organisation afin d’apporter un support optimal. De cette manière, vous aidez vos collègues à atteindre leurs objectifs professionnels. Les points essentiels de ce défi sont les suivants : Gestion et architecture des serveurs : vous maintenez nos serveurs en parfait état, assurez le monitoring des performances, résolvez les problèmes techniques, implémentez des améliorations et veillez à l’architecture IT de Matexi ; Configuration réseau : vous configurez et maintenez les réseaux locaux, les systèmes de téléphonie et d’impression ainsi que d’autres systèmes de communication de données ; Sécurité et sauvegardes : vous effectuez régulièrement des contrôles de sécurité et veillez à la mise en place des sauvegardes ; Support utilisateurs : vous assurez le support de level 2 pour les collègues dans l’utilisation des appareils et des logiciels, et rédigez les manuels nécessaires ; Tendances et évolutions : vous suivez de près les dernières évolutions du domaine et appliquez ces connaissances dans votre travail. À propos de vous Vous êtes un véritable team player , ouvert d’esprit et ne craignez pas de vous tromper ; Vous communiquez aisément en néerlandais (indispensable) ; Vous disposez obligatoirement d’un minimum de 3 ans d’expérience en tant que System Administrator. Vous avez de l’expérience avec Azure et M365 ; Vous possédez une bonne connaissance globale de l’infrastructure IT Microsoft (AD, DNS, GPO, Intune, SQL, PowerShell scripting, etc.) ; La documentation, l’automatisation et la sécurité font partie de votre ADN ; Vous êtes proactif(ve), orienté(e) solutions et n’hésitez pas à mettre la main à la pâte ; Vous avez un esprit analytique tout en gardant une approche pragmatique ; Vous disposez de bonnes compétences en communication . Vous êtes mobile : la fonction implique une présence de 2 jours par semaine au siège social de Waregem, et les 3 autres jours dans nos bureaux de Bruxelles et en Wallonie (Fleurus, Wavre et Liège). Une connaissance de HPE Aruba, des firewalls Palo Alto, de Cisco Meraki, d’ITIL et du Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) sont un plus. Ce que nous vous offrons Autonomie et flexibilité ; Des collègues experts, motivés et prêts à relever des défis ; Un package salarial attractif, adapté à votre expérience et à vos compétences, comprenant une voiture de société et des avantages extralégaux (chèques-repas et écochèques, assurance groupe et hospitalisation, bonus collectif, etc.). Un parcours de formation et de développement sur mesure via la Matexi Academy ; Des journées de travail variées et suffisamment de jours de congé pour recharger vos batteries ; Un emploi au sein d’une entreprise saine : Matexi est un leader du marché avec une vision forte. À propos de nous Depuis sa création en 1945, Matexi permet à chacun d'acquérir une habitation abordable, durable et de qualité. Notre slogan « Bienvenue chez vous » illustre notre volonté de créer des quartiers inspirants propices à l’épanouissement de ceux et celles qui y résident, toutes générations confondues. Matexi possède des bureaux dans chaque province du pays ainsi qu’en Région de Bruxelles-Capitale, au Grand-Duché de Luxembourg et en Pologne (Varsovie). L’entreprise compte plus de 350 collaborateurs. Elle développe actuellement 600 quartiers dans 300 villes et communes et réalise chaque année plus de 1.500 logements et appartements . Questions ? N’hésitez pas à me contacter ! Janis Paulus - HR BP – 0478/27 05 59 Chez Matexi, le travail d’équipe est au cœur de nos valeurs. Chaque avis compte. Nous attachons donc une grande importance à l’identité et à la diversité, afin que vous puissiez, en tant qu’individu unique, contribuer à la création de quartiers inspirants. Cookies needed for this module weren't accepted or are blocked. Please check and review your cookie settings if you want to change this behaviour.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5571012983.scraped: (H/F) DevSecOps Engineer As a DevSecOps Engineer , your role is to: Set up and monitor all the steps, tools, and practices that enable the development, testing, and deployment of software applications at Speos, in order to improve the performance of every stage of the delivery chain. Evolve CI/CD and operational platforms by integrating best practices in automation, observability, and security. You act as a facilitator for developers, as a guarantor of quality, and as a key player in the company’s technical transformation. Your responsibilities As a DevSecOps Engineer , your responsibilities include: CI/CD Platform – Design, Evolution, and Maintenance Design, maintain, and evolve the CI/CD platform to provide developers with a modern, intuitive, and productive tool. Integrate leading technologies (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, ArgoCD…) to optimize pipeline efficiency. Measure and improve pipeline performance (execution time, success/failure rate) to ensure robustness. Deploy Infrastructure as Code (IaC) to version pipelines and environments. Security by Design – Continuous Security Integration (DevSecOps) Maintain and evolve vulnerability scanning tools (source code, libraries and dependencies, Docker images). Implement automated security policies in pipelines to detect vulnerabilities early in the development process. Integrate secret and credential verification in the CI/CD process. Ensure secure secrets management (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager…). Software Quality – Test Automation Ensure the automation of unit tests in CI pipelines. Promote integration and automated security testing. Measure and report test coverage to developers. Automate static and dynamic code analysis (linting, SAST, etc.). Artifact and Registry Management Administer code repositories (GitLab), library repositories (Nexus), and image registries (Nexus) to ensure artifact availability. Implement retention, tagging, cleanup, and security policies to guarantee integrity and version control. Deployment Automation (CD) Build and maintain automated deployment pipelines to accelerate production release cycles. Integrate security or approval gates into production flows to secure releases. Ensure consistency and stability across environments (Dev, Test, Prod). Track and trace each deployment (logs, signatures, audits). Observability, Monitoring, and Performance Deploy and maintain observability tools (logs, traces, metrics) to ensure system visibility. Ensure application and infrastructure monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana, ELK…). Optimize resource usage and detect anomalies. Provide meaningful alerts and facilitate incident diagnosis. Compliance, Audit, and Governance Maintain CI/CD action and access traceability to meet security and audit requirements. Contribute to the preparation of security or quality audits (ISO 27001, GDPR…). Document processes, policies, and security rules to support compliance and transparency. Participate in compliance reviews and risk management. DevSecOps Culture and Team Enablement Support developers in using CI/CD tools and best practices. Lead training sessions or workshops on security awareness (OWASP, secure coding…). Promote a DevSecOps culture based on automation, collaboration, and feedback. Contribute to incident resolution and continuous improvement post-mortems. Continuous Improvement of the Operational Platform Identify business needs and adapt infrastructure accordingly. Drive structural technical evolutions (containers, Kubernetes, GitOps…). Ensure scalability, resilience, and availability of the platform. Proactively suggest relevant emerging technologies for Speos. Your profile As a DevSecOps Engineer , you bring: Minimum 2 years of experience as a DevOps Engineer. Strong knowledge of application security principles and pipelines (DAST, SAST, secret management). Expertise in CI/CD and automation of development workflows. Proficiency in Infrastructure as Code (Terraform). Knowledge of public cloud (AWS). Familiarity with observability and alerting tools (ELK, Grafana). Strong communication and cross-team collaboration skills. Fluency in French and English is a must (Dutch is a plus).

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5619869783.scraped: Building Automation Engineer Are you a hands-on problem solver with a passion for making buildings smarter, greener and more efficient? This is your chance to join a forward-thinking company leading the way in building automation and energy optimisation across Belgium. Clear are looking for a Building Automation Engineer who excels in managing complex projects, solving technical challenges, and ensuring successful delivery. You’ll be responsible for leading the design, coordination, and implementation of advanced automation systems in commercial buildings, hospitals, data centres, and other high-profile facilities, ensuring projects are completed on time, within scope, and to the highest standards. Flemish Region €4,000 - €5,500 Monthly Salary 13 Months Salary Lunch Tokens Bonus Car or Car Allowance Overtime Expenses Training and Development Great Social Environment Responsibilities: Designing and engineering building automation and control system solutions to meet project specifications. Developing system architectures, control strategies and detailed engineering documentation. Programming and configuring controllers, HMIs, and communication protocols (e.g., BACnet, Modbus, KNX). Overseeing factory acceptance testing (FAT), site installation, commissioning and system integration. Collaborating closely with multidisciplinary teams to deliver reliable and optimised automation solutions. Desired Experience: Proven experience in building automation, BMS, HVAC controls or similar. Strong troubleshooting skills and an eye for detail. Comfortable working both independently and as part of a collaborative team. Norwegian and English language skills (both ideal). What's on Offer: Competitive salary and benefits package. Opportunities to work on diverse, high-impact projects across Oslo and beyond. Access to the latest tools and technologies in building automation. A supportive team that values your expertise and helps you grow further. INDEU

Clear are looking for a Building Automation Engineer who excels in managing complex projects, solving technical challenges, and ensuring successful delivery. You’ll be responsible for leading the design, coordination, and implementation of advanced automation systems in commercial buildings, hospitals, data centres, and other high-profile facilities, ensuring projects are completed on time, within scope, and to the highest standards. Flemish Region €4,000 - €5,500 Monthly Salary 13 Months Salary Lunch Tokens Bonus Car or Car Allowance Overtime Expenses Training and Development Great Social Environment Responsibilities: Designing and engineering building automation and control system solutions to meet project specifications. Developing system architectures, control strategies and detailed engineering documentation. Programming and configuring controllers, HMIs, and communication protocols (e.g., BACnet, Modbus, KNX). Overseeing factory acceptance testing (FAT), site installation, commissioning and system integration. Collaborating closely with multidisciplinary teams to deliver reliable and optimised automation solutions. Desired Experience: Proven experience in building automation, BMS, HVAC controls or similar. Strong troubleshooting skills and an eye for detail. Comfortable working both independently and as part of a collaborative team. Norwegian and English language skills (both ideal). What's on Offer: Competitive salary and benefits package. Opportunities to work on diverse, high-impact projects across Oslo and beyond. Access to the latest tools and technologies in building automation. A supportive team that values your expertise and helps you grow further. INDEU

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5618176424.scraped: Job Description Key Responsibilities Lead and support a team of IT technicians and support staff. Coordinate and oversee daily IT operations, ensuring high availability of systems. Manage and improve POS systems, order management platforms, reservation tools, and digital payment solutions specific to the restaurant business. Act as the liaison between IT and restaurant operations to ensure smooth technology integration. Identify process improvement opportunities and lead implementation. Oversee IT support requests and ensure timely resolutions. Develop and maintain documentation for systems, processes, and procedures. Assist in the rollout of new software or hardware across multiple restaurant locations if needed. Ensure network security, data integrity, and compliance with privacy regulations. Provide on-site support in Wilrijk, with possible visits to other branches if applicable. Requirements Competitive salary and benefits. Opportunity to influence and shape IT infrastructure in a modernizing sector. Room for growth and leadership development.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5583162512.scraped: Description This position is part of the FN BBN TOP DevOps team, which defines and implements the digital architecture for key R&D areas in support of a business transformation to achieve product development acceleration/continuous delivery on the longer term. The DevOps Continuous Integration Engineer contributes to the FN BBN DevOps enablement roadmap implementation following the priorities set by the DevOps transformation lead. He or she will be responsible for optimizing the sw integration & test flows within FN BBN. Cooperation with product delivery units and service teams is key part of the job. As an expert, he or she will leverage on and re-use DevOps practices and tools introduced by other BUs and explore new possibilities provided by Open Source SW. #NIFN2026 Responsibilities Own the CI/CD Pipeline: Design, implement, and manage robust and scalable CI/CD pipelines for our software development lifecycle, ensuring smooth and efficient integration and delivery. Champion Containerization and Automation: Leverage your expertise in Docker and Kubernetes to build and manage containerized applications and automate deployment processes. Master Build Systems and Scripting: Utilize your strong scripting skills (Python, Bash) and knowledge of build systems (Make, Bazel) to optimize build processes and implement advanced caching techniques. Thrive in a Linux Environment: Demonstrate your deep understanding of Linux systems, including system administration, networking, and security, to ensure the stability and performance of our CI/CD infrastructure. Collaborate and Problem-Solve: Work closely with development teams to troubleshoot issues, implement best practices, and continuously improve our CI/CD processes and tools. Qualifications Champion CI/CD Automation: Design, develop, and implement automation scripts and solutions to streamline and optimize the entire CI/CD pipeline for FN BBN products. Manage & Harden Environments: Take ownership of build and test environments, ensuring their stability, security, and scalability across physical infrastructure, OpenStack, and Kubernetes. Accelerate Software Delivery: Continuously improve CI availability and cycle times to accelerate the software validation and delivery process for increased efficiency and faster time-to-market. Elevate Code Quality & Testing: Strengthen static code analysis practices and enhance test automation frameworks to ensure high-quality code and robust software releases. Collaborate & Drive Improvements: Partner with product development, cloud infrastructure, and operations teams to identify and implement CI/CD pipeline optimizations and process improvements. Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written (EN). Cross-cultural knowledge and global mindset.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5604120776.scraped: This is where your work makes a difference. At Baxter, we believe every person—regardless of who they are or where they are from—deserves a chance to live a healthy life. It was our founding belief in 1931 and continues to be our guiding principle. We are redefining healthcare delivery to make a greater impact today, tomorrow, and beyond. Our Baxter colleagues are united by our Mission to Save and Sustain Lives. Together, our community is driven by a culture of courage, trust, and collaboration. Every individual is empowered to take ownership and make a meaningful impact. We strive for efficient and effective operations, and we hold each other accountable for delivering exceptional results. Here, you will find more than just a job—you will find purpose and pride. Role: Industrial Automation Engineer Location: Lessines, Belgium Mission: Be a key driver of smart manufacturing at Baxter Lessines. As an Industrial Automation Engineer , you will ensure the reliability, performance, and continuous improvement of automated production systems—supporting 24/7 operations that deliver life‑saving solutions worldwide. What You Will Do: Ensure Operational Continuity Quickly diagnose and resolve automation issues (PLC, drives, etc.) to minimize downtime. Provide expert support to production and maintenance teams, including on-call support when required. Lead Automation Projects Implement automation upgrades and new initiatives. Write and review technical specifications and oversee system validation (IQ/OQ/PQ). Support CAPEX planning and ensure alignment with site automation strategy. Optimize & Innovate Monitor and continuously improve production systems and processes. Propose and deploy new technologies (PLC, HMI, Vision, AI, SCADA). Maintain and update PLC, SCADA, and Vision systems. Collaborate & Train Act as a technical advisor across departments. Train teams and ensure documentation remains up to date. Manage relationships with suppliers and external partners. Ensure Compliance Apply GMP, validation, and Change Control processes. Participate in audits and ensure regulatory compliance. What You Will Bring: Degree in Industrial Engineering or Bachelor’s in Automation / Industrial Computing. 5–10 years’ experience in a similar role within a manufacturing or industrial environment. Strong expertise in Siemens S7 and Allen Bradley systems. Solid knowledge of pneumatics, hydraulics, electricity, and mechanics. Fluent French and good command of English. Key Competencies: Analytical mindset, autonomy, flexibility, strong sense of responsibility, and excellent communication and training skills. Equal Employment Opportunity Baxter is an equal opportunity employer. Baxter evaluates qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, gender, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, disability/handicap status or any other legally protected characteristic. Reasonable Accommodations Baxter is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities globally. If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application or interview process, please click on the link here and let us know the nature of your request along with your contact information. Recruitment Fraud Notice Baxter has discovered incidents of employment scams, where fraudulent parties pose as Baxter employees, recruiters, or other agents, and engage with online job seekers in an attempt to steal personal and/or financial information. To learn how you can protect yourself, review our Recruitment Fraud Notice.

https://www.adzuna.ch/details/5615207740.scraped: * 03 February 2026 * 90 - 100% * Permanent position * English (Fluent), French (Fluent) * Region Genf * Log in, to see estimate from jobup.ch Due to the expansion of our development activities, we are looking for a: Test Automation Engineer. Your missions: ? Implement changes in the existing test frameworks used by testers to verify sub-systems (made of multiple software) by following a strongly regulated process: \\* Document each change writing requirements, design (architecture) \\* Modify the Test Framework source code using Python and Squish \\* Write Test Cases and Procedures that will be executed to verify your changes \\* Ensure the qualification of the Test Framework \\* Analyse and describe issues/bugs in our issues Database \\* Synchronize and update the Test Results Management tool ? Lead by example with practical hands-on experience in testing best practices to support the respective team's development towards various testing activities. Your profile: \\* At least 5 years of experience in a safety critical domain. \\* Proven experience in the writing of software testing. \\* Proven experience documenting (specifying, designing, testing) changes. \\* Strong analytical skills to understand end-user needs and translate them into change descriptions. \\* Expert in Python scripting. \\* Proven experience with test automation tools (ideally Squish, Junit, Robot Framework, etc.). \\* Fluent written and spoken English and French is required, German is a plus.
